markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[We've Met Before](/Feats.aspx?ID=7276) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 81

**Requirements**
You’ve spent at least 1 minute in mutual conversation with a creature.

</column>

---

Your ability to discern information about those you interact with is without peer. Spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320) to gain a [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) skill specific to the required creature for the next 24 hours; you have mythic proficiency with that skill and can use it to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) about the creature, [Decipher Writings](/Actions.aspx?ID=2363) created by the creature, [Earn Income](/Actions.aspx?ID=2364) or [Subsist](/Actions.aspx?ID=2368) in areas ruled by the creature, or perform other appropriate tasks as determined by the GM.
name: We've Met Before
requirement_markdown: You’ve spent at least 1 minute in mutual conversation with a creature.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Become Shadow](/Feats.aspx?ID=7277) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 81

</column>

---

You unleash your mythic power to shroud yourself in darkness and silence to stalk your prey. When you attempt a check to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404) or [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=2405), or when you roll [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) for [initiative](/Rules.aspx?ID=3300), you can spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320) to become [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) and to attempt the check or roll using mythic proficiency.
name: Become Shadow

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Cutting Rebuke](/Feats.aspx?ID=7278) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 82

**Prerequisites**
[demagogue's calling](/MythicCallings.aspx?ID=5) or [thespian's calling](/MythicCallings.aspx?ID=10)

</column>

---

If words could kill, the poet might rule the world. Your words can. You gain the [Bon Mot](/Feats.aspx?ID=6466) skill feat. You can spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320) when rolling the [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) check for Bon Mot to attempt the check at mythic proficiency. If the check succeeds, you can also deal mental damage equal to your level to the target (double on a critical success).
name: Cutting Rebuke

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Godspeed](/Feats.aspx?ID=7279) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 82

</column>

---

Mythic power grants you speed to rival [beasts](/Traits.aspx?ID=547) and [demigods](/Rules.aspx?ID=807). Spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320); for the next minute, you gain a +10-foot status bonus to all your Speeds and are [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=89). You can use the extra action each round to [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305), [Step](/Actions.aspx?ID=2304), or [Leap](/Actions.aspx?ID=2298).
name: Godspeed

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Prescience](/Feats.aspx?ID=7280) <actions string="Free Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
<trait label="Prediction" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=674" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 82

**Trigger**
You’re about to roll [initiative](/Rules.aspx?ID=30).

</column>

---

Mythic power floods through your mind, filling you with visions of possible futures, events that might or might not happen, and warnings of things that might be. Spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320) and roll your [initiative](/Rules.aspx?ID=3300) check using mythic proficiency. Until the end of your first turn, you have a +1 status bonus to AC and all saves.
name: Prescience

<title

    pfs="Standard"

[Read The Wind](/Feats.aspx?ID=7281) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 82

**Prerequisites**
[Hunter's Calling](/MythicCallings.aspx?ID=8)

</column>

---

You can derive meaning from the tiniest details when tracking an enemy. Whenever you succeed at a check to [Track](/Actions.aspx?ID=2408) at [mythic proficiency](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320), you can immediately attempt a check to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) about the creature you’re tracking, also at mythic proficiency and with a suitable skill determined by the GM.
name: Read The Wind

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Unending Subsistence](/Feats.aspx?ID=7283) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 82

</column>

---

Your mythic power allows you to take a small amount of food and replicate it near endlessly. When you attempt a [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) or [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) check to [Subsist](/Actions.aspx?ID=2368), you can spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320) to attempt the check at mythic proficiency. Succeeding at the check always provides enough food and shelter for you and up to a dozen other creatures. On a critical success, all creatures you obtain food and shelter for gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Fortitude saving throws for the next 24 hours.
name: Unending Subsistence

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Divert Destiny](/Feats.aspx?ID=7285) <actions string="Free Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 82

**Trigger**
An attack or effect would reduce you to 0 Hit Points or kill you outright.

</column>

---

You defy the fate before you, calling on wells of mythic vitality to sustain your life and allow you to persevere. You expend 1 [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320) and survive the triggering attack or effect, lose the [wounded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=99) or [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=69) conditions entirely, don’t increase your [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67) condition, and are conscious and standing with a number of Hit Points equal to 10 + your level, regardless of how many Hit Points you had before.
name: Divert Destiny

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Fling Into action](/Feats.aspx?ID=7286) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 82

</column>

---

You release mythic power to turn disaster into opportunity and hurl yourself into an advantageous position. When you attempt a Reflex save, you can spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320) to attempt the save at mythic proficiency. If the check is successful, you immediately fling yourself up to 30 feet in a direction of your choice, or up to 60 feet on a critical success. When flinging yourself in this manner, you can move the entire distance in a straight horizontal or vertical line, or you can move 5 feet horizontally for every 10 feet you move vertically. For example, if you got a critical success using Fling Into Action while [Grabbing an Edge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2313) as you fall from the middle of a cliff, you could hurl yourself 40 feet up the cliffside and 20 feet forward onto a plateau at the top of the cliff.
name: Fling Into action

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Mythic Counterspell](/Feats.aspx?ID=7287) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 82

**Prerequisites**
[Quick Recognition](/Feats.aspx?ID=5197); ability to [cast spells](/Rules.aspx?ID=3144) from spell slots,; [Sage's Calling](/MythicCallings.aspx?ID=9)

**Trigger**
A creature Casts a Spell, you’ve successfully Recognized the Spell, and you have either a prepared spell or an unexpended spell slot of equal or greater rank.

</column>

---

You quench and counter your enemy’s magic with potent spellcraft amplified and enhanced by carefully deployed mythic power. Spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320), and expend a prepared spell or unexpended spell slot of the same rank as the triggering spell or higher. You lose your spell slot as if you’d cast the triggering spell. You then attempt to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=3280) the triggering spell using mythic proficiency to determine your spellcasting proficiency bonus for the counteract check.

<title level="2">Mythic Counterspell leads to...</title>
[Steal Magic](/Feats.aspx?ID=7294)
name: Mythic Counterspell
prerequisite_markdown: [Quick Recognition](/Feats.aspx?ID=5197); ability to [cast spells](/Rules.aspx?ID=3144) from spell slots,; [Sage's Calling](/MythicCallings.aspx?ID=9)

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Repel Assault](/Feats.aspx?ID=7288) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 83

**Prerequisites**
[Guardian's Calling](/MythicCallings.aspx?ID=6)

**Trigger**
An enemy’s attack would deal damage equal to or greater than your level to you or an adjacent ally.

</column>

---

Your Calling demands that you defy danger and injury to yourself or those who rely on you while allowing you to respond to the originators of such aggression with ferocious and unrivaled fury. Spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320); you gain [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) to the triggering attack equal to your level. If you [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) the enemy that made the attack before the end of your next turn, you can make that Strike at mythic proficiency. This benefit applies only to the first Strike you make against that enemy.
name: Repel Assault

    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Unrivaled Retort](/Feats.aspx?ID=7289) <actions string="Reaction" />

<traits>

</column>

---

You defy the death and destruction around you while using it to fuel your mythic power, spurring you on to acts of incredible speed and empowering a mighty ranged counterattack. Spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320) and attempt the triggering save at mythic proficiency. If the save is successful, you can immediately [reload](/Rules.aspx?ID=2196) and make a ranged [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) targeting the originator of the triggering effect.
name: Unrivaled Retort

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Arc of Destruction](/Feats.aspx?ID=7290) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 83

**Trigger**
An enemy you’re observing targets you with a ranged weapon [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306).

</column>

---

You redirect an incoming weapon or projectile and send your enemy’s own attack roaring back at them, increasing its accuracy with a surge of mythic power. Spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320); you gain a +4 status bonus to AC against the triggering Strike. If the Strike fails, you immediately snatch the weapon or ammunition used for the attack out of the air and launch it back at the enemy, using mythic proficiency for your Strike. If the attack was made with a [thrown](/Traits.aspx?ID=711) weapon, add the weapon’s [runes](/Rules.aspx?ID=3162) and other effects to the attack as normal. If the triggering attack was made with a piece of ammunition that you’re wielding an appropriate weapon for, you can apply your weapon’s runes and other effects to the attack.
name: Arc of Destruction
search_markdown:
<traits>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Mythic Allies](/Feats.aspx?ID=7291) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 83

**Prerequisites**
ability to cast spells from slots, at least one [summon](/Traits.aspx?ID=520) spell in your spellbook or repertoire

</column>

---

You channel mythic power through your body and into a summoned ally, temporarily reinforcing them with a shared fraction of your mythic potential. Spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320); the [summoned](/Traits.aspx?ID=706) creature gains a +2 status bonus to its attack rolls, a +2 status bonus to all of its defenses, and a number of additional Hit Points equal to your level that last for the duration of its summoning. The next [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) made by the summoned creature after you use this ability gains a +4 circumstance bonus to the attack roll.
name: Mythic Allies
prerequisite_markdown: ability to cast spells from slots, at least one [summon](/Traits.aspx?ID=520) spell in your spellbook or repertoire

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Mythic Magic](/Feats.aspx?ID=7292) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 83

</column>

---

You convert your mythic power into a magical effect. When you gain this feat, choose up to three spells of 1st or 2nd rank that take no more than 3 actions to cast and that are either instantaneous or have a duration of 10 minutes or less. You can spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320) to cast any of those spells for their normal action cost and use mythic proficiency to determine your [spell attack rolls](/Rules.aspx?ID=2292) and spell DCs, and the spells are automatically heightened to a rank equal to half your level (rounded up). If you don’t normally have the ability to cast spells, you use Charisma as your spellcasting attribute modifier; otherwise, you use the spellcasting ability associated with your spellcasting class. You gain the [Cast a Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734) activity only for spells cast with this feat; you don’t otherwise gain the typical benefits of being a spellcaster.

**Special** At 14th level, add one spell of 3rd rank for you to cast using this ability. At 20th level, add one 4th-rank spell.
name: Mythic Magic

[Mythic Refocus](/Feats.aspx?ID=7293) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>

<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

**Prerequisites**
You have a [focus](/Rules.aspx?ID=285) pool.

</column>

---

As your ability to control your mythic power grows, you learn to channel it to restore and reinforce other forms of power at your command. Spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320); you immediately recover all your [Focus](/Rules.aspx?ID=285) Points, filling your focus pool to its current maximum.
name: Mythic Refocus

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Steal Magic](/Feats.aspx?ID=7294) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 83

**Prerequisites**
[Mythic Counterspell](/Feats.aspx?ID=7287)

</column>

---

You don’t just counter your enemy’s magic, you instantly master it and make it your own. When you successfully counterspell an enemy’s spell using Mythic Counterspell, you can learn that spell as long as it’s on the spell list for your tradition. If you have a spellbook, the spell is instantly scribed into the book, creating new blank pages if needed. If you have a spell repertoire, you can instantly retrain any spell currently in your repertoire to the countered spell, though if the spell you replace is one granted to you by a class feature (such as an animist’s apparition spells or a sorcerer’s bloodline spells), the spell reverts to the one normally granted by that class feature during your next daily preparations.
name: Steal Magic

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Unbelievable Interception](/Feats.aspx?ID=7296) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 84

**Trigger**
An enemy you’re [observing](/Conditions.aspx?ID=84) attempts to [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) an ally you can also observe.

</column>

---

You move with unbelievable alacrity to intercept an enemy’s attack. Spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320); [stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) twice directly toward the triggering enemy. If you end within the attack’s range or reach, you become the target of the triggering Strike, using the original attack roll and comparing the result to your AC. If the enemy’s attack fails and the enemy is within your reach, you can immediately attempt a melee Strike against the enemy at mythic proficiency.
name: Unbelievable Interception

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Become Destiny](/Feats.aspx?ID=7297) <actions string="Free Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 84

</column>

---

You defy any external force blocking your mythic purpose. Spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320), and remove any one condition you’re afflicted with, end the ongoing effects of any one spell you’re currently affected by, or free yourself from the effects of any one [trap](/Traits.aspx?ID=715) or [hazard](/Rules.aspx?ID=2846) (such as a [snare](/Traits.aspx?ID=809) or [bottomless pit](/Hazards.aspx?ID=11)). When freeing yourself from a hazard in this way, you immediately move to the nearest safe location adjacent to the hazard.
name: Become Destiny

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Mythic Containment](/Feats.aspx?ID=7301) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 84

**Prerequisites**
[Artisan's Calling](/MythicCallings.aspx?ID=2)

</column>

---

You infuse a receptacle, such as a normal bag, sack, or box, with mythic power, transforming it into a prison into which you can command a creature. Spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320), and attempt a [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38), [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39), or [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) check at mythic proficiency against the Will DC of a creature within 30 feet to trick, convince, or command it into entering the receptacle. The receptacle can hold a creature of any size, and the creature enters the receptacle if your check is successful. The creature is [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77), is [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1, and has to hold its breath or start [suffocating](/Rules.aspx?ID=2439). A creature trapped in the receptacle can attempt to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296); use the skill you used to lure the creature into the bag for the DC, and calculate the DC using mythic proficiency. The trapped creature is automatically freed after 1 minute, but you can spend an additional Mythic Point as a free action to prolong the duration of the containment for an additional minute when it would expire.
name: Mythic Containment

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Unbreaking Castle](/Feats.aspx?ID=7303) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 84

**Requirements**
You’re wielding a [shield](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=12) or a weapon with the [parry](/Traits.aspx?ID=667) trait.

</column>

---

You’re the bulwark that doesn’t break. Spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320), and [Raise a Shield](/Actions.aspx?ID=2316) or position the required weapon to parry; for the next minute, whenever you Raise a Shield or position a weapon to parry (including when you first use this ability), all allies you can see gain the shield or weapon’s circumstance bonus to AC. When your shield is raised or your weapon is positioned to parry while this effect is active, enemies treat all squares within your reach as [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366). When using the [Shield Block](/Feats.aspx?ID=5212) reaction, after the block is complete, you can attempt a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) with a [shield bash](/Weapons.aspx?ID=395) or an attached weapon on the required shield (such as a [shield boss](/Weapons.aspx?ID=396) or [shield spikes](/Weapons.aspx?ID=397)) against an adjacent enemy at mythic proficiency.

The first time each round an enemy misses you with a melee Strike while your weapon is positioned to parry, you can Strike them with that weapon at mythic proficiency as a reaction.
name: Unbreaking Castle
requirement_markdown: You’re wielding a [shield](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=12) or a weapon with the [parry](/Traits.aspx?ID=667) trait.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 12"
    pfs=""
>
[Apocalypse Rider Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7304) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Destiny" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=838" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 107

**Archetype** [Apocalypse Rider](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=287)

</column>

---

In the darkest hour of the night, a fearsome and terrible steed arrives as a gift from a powerful daemonic patron. You gain an apocalypse mount, which is loyal and follows your commands as long as you work to spread death and destruction. Your apocalypse mount can be any [mature animal companion](/Rules.aspx?ID=2117) with the [mount](/Feats.aspx?ID=2113) special ability, though it is obviously touched by daemonic forces. Choose whether your apocalypse mount is a [nimble](/Companions.aspx?Type=Advancement&ID=7) or [savage](/Companions.aspx?Type=Advancement&ID=8) animal companion. During an encounter, even if you don’t use the [Command an Animal](/Actions.aspx?ID=2400) action, your apocalypse mount can still use 1 action on your turn to [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) or [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306).

You also gain the anathema “You forswear any plans to cheat death or build something that will last beyond your own death.” If you break this anathema, you lose access to abilities granted by this archetype until you atone or make amends.

**Special** If you already have an animal companion with the mount special ability, it becomes your apocalypse mount as daemonic energies infuse its form. In such a case, one of your companion’s unarmed attacks increases one die size.

<title level="2">Apocalypse Rider Dedication leads to...</title>
[Behold, A Pale Horse](/Feats.aspx?ID=7305), [Contagious Spell](/Feats.aspx?ID=7315), [Deathless Servant](/Feats.aspx?ID=7316), [History of Violence](/Feats.aspx?ID=7306), [Immortal Ruin](/Feats.aspx?ID=7319), [Jousting Mount](/Feats.aspx?ID=7307), [Memory of Nothing](/Feats.aspx?ID=7317), [Night Terror](/Feats.aspx?ID=7310), [One Among The Masses](/Feats.aspx?ID=7308), [Putrefaction](/Feats.aspx?ID=7309), [Soul Vessel Mount](/Feats.aspx?ID=7318), [Steal Death](/Feats.aspx?ID=7311), [To War!](/Feats.aspx?ID=7312), [Virulent Strike](/Feats.aspx?ID=7313), [Wither Away](/Feats.aspx?ID=7314)
name: Apocalypse Rider Dedication

[History of Violence](/Feats.aspx?ID=7306) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
<trait label="Void" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=510" />

---

Your attacks bring with them echoes of previous battles, calling the unquiet victims of your most brutal acts to savage your opponents. Make a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306). If this Strike hits, you deal additional [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) damage equal to your key attribute modifier to the target and all enemies within 30 feet. If your attack is a critical hit, you deal twice as much damage instead, and the target of your Strike takes [persistent void damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) equal to your key attribute modifier as your fallen foes attempt to drag the target into oblivion with them. This Strike counts as two attacks for calculating your multiple attack penalty.
name: History of Violence

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 14"
    pfs=""
>
[One Among The Masses](/Feats.aspx?ID=7308) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 107

**Prerequisites**
[Apocalypse Rider Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7304)

</column>

---

It will take more than a gathered mass of foes to take you down. You gain [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) equal to your level to damage dealt by [swarms](/Traits.aspx?ID=707), [troops](/Traits.aspx?ID=367), and creatures who occupy the same space as you. In addition, you can briefly menace large groups of enemies to step aside. You gain the Overawe Crowd activity.

**Overawe Crowd** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Requirements** You are sharing the same space as a [swarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=707) or are adjacent to a [troop](/Traits.aspx?ID=367); **Effect** Spend 1 Mythic Point and attempt an [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) check at mythic proficiency against the Will DC of the required swarm or troop. On a failure, the target Strides in a direction of your choosing and you move along with it. On a success, the target Strides twice in a direction of your choosing and you can decide whether or not to move along with it. No matter the result, the target is stunned 1 and temporarily immune to your Overawe Crowd for 24 hours.
name: One Among The Masses

You spoil the food and water nearby, even within your foes’ bodies. Spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320). Enemies within 60 feet of you must succeed at a Fortitude save against your class DC or spell DC (whichever is higher) or become [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1. You also cause edible food to rot and potable water to turn brackish within the area. Choose a number of [potions](/Traits.aspx?ID=672) or alchemical [elixirs](/Traits.aspx?ID=589) within the area equal to your key attribute modifier and attempt a [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) against each of them using [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) or [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) at mythic proficiency. Your counteract rank is half your level. If you succeed, the elixir or potion spoils and becomes a mundane item.
name: Putrefaction

[Steal Death](/Feats.aspx?ID=7311) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Death" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=571" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />

**Archetype** [Apocalypse Rider](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=287)

[Apocalypse Rider Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7304)

**Trigger**
Another creature within 30 feet gains the [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67) or [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=69) condition or their doomed or dying condition increases.

</column>

---

As a harbinger of daemonkind, you gain authority to delay the death of others if it serves your purposes, and in doing so, you bolster the well-being of both you and your mount to allow you sow further havoc. The triggering creature reduces their doomed or dying condition by 1. For the next minute, you and your apocalypse mount gain [fast healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=62) 5 as long as you are within 10 feet of each other. If you use this reaction again within that minute, you and your mount’s fast healing increases by 1, but the duration isn’t increased.
name: Steal Death

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 16"
    pfs=""
>
[To War!](/Feats.aspx?ID=7312) <actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 108

**Archetype** [Apocalypse Rider](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=287)

**Frequency**
once per 10 minutes

**Prerequisites**
[Apocalypse Rider Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7304)

**Requirements**
You are riding your apocalypse [mount](/Actions.aspx?ID=2314).

</column>

---

You inspire your allies to greater battle prowess while trampling enemies in your way. You Command your apocalypse mount to [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) up to twice its Speed (or to [Burrow](/Actions.aspx?ID=2310), [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=2374), [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312), or [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=2381), if it has the corresponding movement type), moving through the space of any foes in your path up to one size smaller than your apocalypse mount. Your mount deals damage equal to one of its unarmed attack [Strikes](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) to each creature whose space you move through (basic Reflex save against your apocalypse mount’s [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) DC). On a critical failure, the creature also becomes [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) until the end of your next turn. You can damage a given creature only once during this movement.

In addition, all allies who witnessed your charge gain a number of temporary Hit Points equal to your level and a +2 status bonus to damage against any foe damaged by your apocalypse mount this round. This has the [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727) trait. These benefits last until the end of your next turn.
name: To War!

If your attack hits, the target must attempt a new saving throw against the highest-level [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578) or [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) that is currently affecting it, possibly progressing it to a higher stage even if the [affliction](/Rules.aspx?ID=2389)’s interval hasn’t passed. If the target successfully saves or critically saves against the affliction, the affliction’s stage isn’t reduced. If your attack was a critical hit, the target takes a –4 circumstance penalty to the save.
name: Virulent Strike

markdown:
**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 108 **Archetype** [Apocalypse Rider](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=287) **Frequency** once per 10 minutes **Prerequisites** [Apocalypse Rider Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7304); [Battle Harbinger Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7505) --- You hasten the passage of time for a living creature, prematurely aging them into decrepitude. A living creature within 60 feet takes 14d6 [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) damage and a part of its body you chose withers, taking the following penalties depending on the result of their a Fortitude saving throw against your class DC or spell DC, whichever is higher.
  • **Arms** The target’s arms grow weak. The target becomes [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 1.
  • **Head** The target has trouble thinking straight. The target becomes [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1.
  • **Legs** The target’s legs buckle with every movement. The target takes a –10-foot status penalty to its Speeds.
  • **Torso** The target’s skin becomes thin and papery. The target gains [weakness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2317) 10 to slashing damage.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target takes half damage and the withering penalty lasts until the end of your next turn.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage and the withering penalty lasts for 1 minute.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes double damage, and you can choose a second part of the body to wither. Both penalties last for 1 minute.
name: Wither Away

>
[Deathless Servant](/Feats.aspx?ID=7316) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Healing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=623" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />

---

The servants of apocalypse know that it is impossible to cheat death, but your will is sufficient to help you outlive even your strongest enemies. The first time each day you would be reduced to 0 Hit Points but not immediately killed, you avoid being knocked out and instead of taking the damage, you regain a number of Hit Points equal to twice your level. Your [wounded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=99) condition increases by 1.

 In addition, whenever you have the wounded condition, you gain a status bonus to your Armor Class equal to your wounded condition value and a status bonus to the damage of your [Strikes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) equal to twice your wounded condition value.
name: Deathless Servant

markdown:
**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 109 **Archetype** [Apocalypse Rider](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=287) **Prerequisites** [Apocalypse Rider Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7304) --- With a look and a gesture, you turn a thinking creature’s mind against itself, causing the creature to struggle to remember how to perform more complex actions. Choose a target within 30 feet and spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320). The target must attempt a Will save against your class DC or spell DC (whichever is higher) at mythic proficiency. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** For the next 3 rounds, if the target performs an activity that requires three or more actions, they take 12d8 [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) damage.
**Failure** For the next 3 rounds, if the target performs an activity that requires two or more actions, they take 12d8 mental damage.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the target is also [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) 1 as its mind struggles to function.
name: Memory of Nothing

With a look and a gesture, you turn a thinking creature’s mind against itself, causing the creature to struggle to remember how to perform more complex actions.
</summary>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 18"
    pfs=""
>
[Soul Vessel Mount](/Feats.aspx?ID=7318) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 109

**Archetype** [Apocalypse Rider](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=287)

**Prerequisites**
[Apocalypse Rider Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7304)

</column>

---

Your mount has a supernatural hunger for mortal souls that you can feed. Once per day, you can cast [_seize soul_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1664) as a divine innate spell. The spell gains a cost of 1 [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320) but you can use your apocalypse mount as the item in which you store the soul. Your apocalypse mount counts as an [artifact](/Traits.aspx?ID=537) for the purpose of the spell. At any point while your apocalypse mount contains a soul, you can [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) the spell to allow your apocalypse mount to consume the soul. It regains a number of Hit Points equal to twice your level. For the next minute, it also gains a +20-foot status bonus to its Speed and its unarmed attack [Strikes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) are made at mythic proficiency.
name: Soul Vessel Mount

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 20"
    pfs=""
>
[Immortal Ruin](/Feats.aspx?ID=7319) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 109

**Archetype** [Apocalypse Rider](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=287)

**Prerequisites**
[Apocalypse Rider Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7304)

</column>

---

Your commitment to ending all life earns you a measure of immortality, setting the stage for you to become a true Rider of the Apocalypse, possibly supplanting one of the Four. You gain the [daemon](/Traits.aspx?ID=568), [fiend](/Traits.aspx?ID=600), and [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) traits, and so does your apocalypse mount, but your fates are ever more entwined. As long as your apocalypse mount lives, you don’t age and whenever your [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67) or [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=69) condition would increase to 4 or more, it doesn’t increase. If you would be killed in any other way, you are instead reduced to 0 Hit Points, become [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95), and if your apocalypse mount is conscious, it instantly teleports to your side, picks you up, and attempts to flee the scene of danger. 

While you are conscious and within 30 feet of your apocalypse mount, it can’t be killed. If your apocalypse mount is reduced to 0 Hit Points, it immediately [stabilizes](/Rules.aspx?ID=1381) and its dying condition can’t increase. If its doomed condition would increase to 4 or more, it doesn’t increase. However, if you move more than 30 feet away from your apocalypse mount or go unconscious during this time, your apocalypse mount loses these benefits and might die.

Finally, when a living creature within 60 feet of you dies, it must attempt a Will saving throw against your class DC or spell DC, whichever is higher. This is an [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631) effect. On a failure, that creature can’t be brought back to life for 24 hours. On a critical failure, you can chose to have that creature’s soul coalesce into a fist-sized soul gem (Hardness 2, HP 8) that appears in that creature’s space. That creature’s soul is trapped within the gem for 1 week, though you can make the gem permanent with a 10-minute ritual. Destroying the gem frees the soul within but doesn’t return the deceased creature to life. If a caster attempts to return to life a creature whose soul is trapped within a soul gem, they fail unless they succeed at a [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) check against your class DC or spell DC, whichever is higher. A success causes the soul gem to shatter, allowing the creature to be returned to life as normal for the spell. Daemons and other fiends prize these soul gems, and they’re generally worth an amount relative to the level of a gem’s captive soul.
name: Immortal Ruin

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 12"
    pfs=""
>
[Archfiend Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7320) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Destiny" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=838" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 110

**Archetype** [Archfiend](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=288)

</column>

---

Driven by your Calling, you've begun to make plans for your own personal realm from whence you're destined to rule over an army of fiends. Name your realm and describe its eventual appearance. Whether it contains raging hellfire, ponds of caustic acid, or banks of poisonous fog, your realm should be marked by distinctive [terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=3108). Select one of the following damage types associated with your realm's environmental dangers: [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524), [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), or [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669). This is your realm's damage type. You gain [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) to the selected damage type equal to half your level. If you already have resistance to this damage type, increase your resistance by 5. The certainty of your will is such that you can temporarily bring forth a portion of your realm into this world. You gain the Manifest Realm ability. Other feats allow you to further manipulate your manifested realm. 

Manifest Realm <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Effect** You corrupt the surrounding area into a manifestation of your realm in a 20-foot-radius burst centered on you. Your manifested realm counts as [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) for your enemies, and your enemies take a –1 penalty to Fortitude saving throws while in your manifested realm. You can Sustain your manifested realm once per round on subsequent rounds, and if you do not, the radius of your manifested realm is reduced by 5 feet. Your realm remains manifested for a maximum of 1 minute, and you can Dismiss your manifested realm. If your manifested realm's radius is ever reduced to 0 feet or if you move beyond 100 feet from the borders of your manifested realm, your manifested realm is automatically Dismissed.

<title level="2">Archfiend Dedication leads to...</title>
[Brandish Authority](/Feats.aspx?ID=7327), [Don thy Fervor](/Feats.aspx?ID=7321), [Fiend Eternal](/Feats.aspx?ID=7330), [Imprison Foe](/Feats.aspx?ID=7328), [Lord of the Fiends](/Feats.aspx?ID=7322), [Manipulate Realm](/Feats.aspx?ID=7323), [Marshall Fiendish Forces](/Feats.aspx?ID=7324), [Profane Bargain](/Feats.aspx?ID=7329), [Realm Strider](/Feats.aspx?ID=7325), [Seat of Power](/Feats.aspx?ID=7326)
name: Archfiend Dedication

    level="1"
    right="Feat 14"
    pfs=""
>
[Manipulate Realm](/Feats.aspx?ID=7323) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 111

**Prerequisites**
[Archfiend Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7320)

</column>

---

Thanks to your continued belief in your ultimate transformation, you have learned greater control over your manifested realm. If you spend a Mythic Point when you Manifest your Realm, you can use the Drain Realm, Spread Realm, and Unleash Realm free actions.

 **Drain Realm** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Trigger** Your previous action was to Sustain your manifested realm; **Requirements** Your manifested realm has a radius of at least 10 feet; Effect You siphon power from your manifested realm to bolster your physical form. Decrease your manifested realm's radius by 10 feet. You regain a number of Hit Points equal to your level and each of your Strikes made before the end of your turn deal an additional 1d8 damage of your realm's damage type.

 **Spread Realm** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Trigger** Your previous action was to Sustain your manifested realm; **Effect** Your manifested realm further corrupts more of your surroundings. Increase your manifested realm's radius by 10 feet.

 **Unleash Realm** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Trigger** Your previous action was to Sustain your manifested realm; **Effect** You punish those who transgress upon your realm. An enemy within your realm takes 4d8 damage of your realm's damage type with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save against your class DC or spell DC, whichever is higher.
name: Manipulate Realm

[Marshall Fiendish Forces](/Feats.aspx?ID=7324) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>

You have been in contact with a retinue of [fiends](/Traits.aspx?ID=600), brokering all manner of deals so that they may eventually serve you. When you [Manifest your Realm](/Actions.aspx?ID=2986), you can choose to have one such fiend come to your aid. This fiend is a [common](/Traits.aspx?ID=557) fiend of level 11 or lower, which you select when you take this feat. The fiend appears in a place of your choosing within your manifested realm and has the [minion](/Traits.aspx?ID=653) trait. They remain for as long as your realm is manifested, until they’re reduced to 0 Hit Points, or until you command them to leave, at which point they immediately vanish and return to their place of origin. 

At 20th level, you can choose a common fiend of level 15 or lower to appear instead when you Manifest your Realm.
name: Marshall Fiendish Forces

You can cast [_translocate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) as a 4th-rank [divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2) innate spell at will. <title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 16"
    pfs=""
>
[Seat of Power](/Feats.aspx?ID=7326) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 112

**Archetype** [Archfiend](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=288)

**Prerequisites**
[Archfiend Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7320)

</column>

---

Your sheer determination has created a demiplane that houses a grand residence which will serve as the heart of your planned realm. You determine the appearance of this seat of power, such as a duskwood longhouse, a crumbling stone castle, an ice tower, or a pagoda constructed from the solidified souls of the damned. You can enter your realm using the Enter Seat of Power activity. You and any creatures you choose gain three times as many Hit Points when [resting](/Rules.aspx?ID=2601) within your seat of power. Once per day while within your seat of power, you can cast [_cleanse affliction_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1467) as a 4th-rank innate [divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2) spell or [_sound body_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1679) as a 4th-rank innate divine spell. You can target only yourself with these spells. 

**Enter Seat of Power** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [exploration](/Skills.aspx?ID=23), [mythic](/Traits.aspx?ID=835)) **Effect** You focus your mind on your seat of power, then tap into this connection to transport yourself to its demiplane. This process takes 10 minutes. You can bring up to 10 willing creatures with you when you Enter Seat of Power; these creatures must remain within 30 feet of you for the duration of the activity.

 You can remain in your seat of power for as long as you desire. Any creature that you bring to your seat of power in this way (including you) can depart the demiplane as a three-action activity that has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) trait. They return to the location they were in when they Entered your Seat of Power, or the nearest unoccupied space if that location is occupied.
name: Seat of Power

>
[Brandish Authority](/Feats.aspx?ID=7327) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Auditory" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=541" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
<trait label="Visual" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=727" />

**Prerequisites**
[Archfiend Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7320)

</column>

---

With a terrifying pronouncement, you manifest a visible sign of your authority and fiendish power, such as a crown of flame upon your brow, a scepter forged of solidified souls, or a throne comprised of a dozen dragon skulls. This object appears in your space or in your hand, as appropriate, and remains for 1 minute. When your sign of power appears, attempt an [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) check to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=2395) each enemy within 30 feet who can see or hear you. These Demoralize attempts don’t take any penalty for not sharing a language. As long as your sign of power remains manifested, your enemies can’t reduce the value of their [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) condition below 1.

You can spend 1 [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320) as part of performing this action; if you do, frightened creatures grovel before you, in awe of your terrible power. At the beginning of their turn, a creature that has the frightened condition from Brandish Authority must attempt a Will saving throw against your class DC or spell DC, whichever is higher. On failure, they must Drop Prone. On a critical failure, they must [Drop Prone](/Actions.aspx?ID=2295) and can’t Stand this turn.
name: Brandish Authority

markdown:
**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 112 **Archetype** [Archfiend](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=288) **Prerequisites** [Archfiend Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7320) **Requirements** Your last action dealt damage to a creature. --- Rather than grant your foes the mercy of a swift death, you can consign them to suffer in an extradimensional prison with an appearance of your choosing. Spend 1 [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320) to force a creature that you dealt damage to with your last action to attempt a Will saving throw against your class DC or spell DC, whichever is higher. Regardless of the result, the target is temporarily immune to your Imprison Foe for 24 hours. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target is [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) 2.
**Failure** The target is transported into a cell within your extradimensional dungeon for 1 minute. It is unable to leave or damage the cell in any way. [Teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710) effects can’t carry the target outside the cell unless they can also traverse the planes, such as [_interplanar teleport_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1576). At the end of each of its turns, it can attempt a new Will save to reduce the remaining duration it is imprisoned by 1 round or end it entirely on a critical success. When the duration ends, the target returns to the space it occupied when it was imprisoned, or to the nearest space if the original is now filled.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the target is [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) for the first round it is imprisoned.
name: Imprison Foe
requirement_markdown: Your last action dealt damage to a creature.

<summary>
Rather than grant your foes the mercy of a swift death, you can consign them to suffer in an extradimensional prison with an appearance of your choosing.
</summary>
summary_markdown: Rather than grant your foes the mercy of a swift death, you can consign them to suffer in an extradimensional prison with an appearance of your choosing.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 18"
    pfs=""
>
[Profane Bargain](/Feats.aspx?ID=7329) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=579" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=647" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 113

**Archetype** [Archfiend](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=288)

**Prerequisites**
[Archfiend Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7320)

</column>

---

You can strike a profane bargain with a willing, sapient creature. That creature promises to never take [hostile actions](/Rules.aspx?ID=2251) against you, to never [lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=2389) to you, to never speak of this bargain, and to either perform, or refrain from performing, one other specified task, such as paying you a specified sum every year, gifting you their first-born child, or never speaking. 

If the target accepts, they verbally make a request. You can choose to twist their request by selecting specific details, as long as you still fulfill the requirements of their request. If the request could be granted by a [_wish_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=125) ritual, you spend 1 Mythic Point and the request is magically granted. 

As long as the bargain persists, you always know the target’s location, can communicate telepathically with the target at any distance, and can see through the target’s senses by taking a single action that has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) trait. Once per day, you can take control of the target as an activity that takes three actions and has the concentrate trait. When you do, they become [controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=64) by you for 10 minutes. They have no memory of their time spent under your control.

If the target ever breaks the terms of this bargain, you immediately select one of the following punishments: they die, they die and you possess their soul, they become permanently controlled by you, or they become imprisoned in a cell within your realm’s dungeon (you must have the [Imprison Foe](/Feats.aspx?ID=7330) feat to select this final option).

A target can only enter into a Profane Bargain with you once—not even the breaking of this bargain, death, a _wish_ ritual, or divine intervention frees them of this limitation. You can’t make a Profane Bargain with yourself.
name: Profane Bargain

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 20"
    pfs=""
>
[Fiend Eternal](/Feats.aspx?ID=7330) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 113

**Archetype** [Archfiend](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=288)

**Prerequisites**
[Archfiend Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7320)

</column>

---

Through all your machinations and accumulation of power, you have completed your apotheosis and become a true archfiend. You gain the [fiend](/Traits.aspx?ID=600) and [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) traits and are immune to your realm’s damage type. You don’t age. Your planned realm becomes a complete reality, forming within a section of [Hell](/Planes.aspx?ID=42), the [Outer Rifts](/Planes.aspx?ID=45), [the Netherworld](/Planes.aspx?ID=36), its own fiendish demiplane, or some other planar space of your choosing. If you have the [Seat of Power](/Feats.aspx?ID=7328) feat, that seat of power is grafted onto your realm and the [Enter Seat of Power](/Actions.aspx?ID=2988) activity transports you to any place within your realm; otherwise, you can enter and exit your realm in a similar fashion with a brief ritual that takes 10 minutes (though you can’t bring anyone with you).

Whenever you would die, you are instead reduced to 0 Hit Points, become [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95), and are immediately transported to your seat of power within your realm. After 1 hour (or 1 round if you have the Seat of Power feat), you regain 5 Hit Points and reawaken. You can’t leave your realm until you’re fully healed or 1 week passes (whichever comes first).

In addition, you gain a small group of fiendish followers who reside in your realm. They never leave your realm (though one might occasionally come to your aid if you have the [Marshal Fiendish Forces](/Feats.aspx?ID=7324) feat), but they constantly spy on the mortal world to keep you informed. You are telepathically linked to these followers and can choose to speak with the group, or a specific fiend, as a three-action activity that has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) trait. In doing so, you can avail yourself of the fiends’ surveillance to learn about the world or current events. You can either [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) on any topic or [Gather Information](/Actions.aspx?ID=2391). If you critically fail to do either, you get a failure instead.
name: Fiend Eternal

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 12"
    pfs=""
>
[Ascended Celestial Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7331) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Destiny" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=838" />
<trait label="Fortune" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=612" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 115

**Archetype** [Ascended Celestial](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=289)

</column>

---

Your Calling has driven you to perform mortal deeds that will result in your ascension to the ranks of celestials. You’re headstrong and determined and hardly waver in your resolve; once per hour, you can roll twice and use the higher result on a Will saving throw. If you ever become [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63), rather than attack wildly, you become stubbornly immobile, wasting all your actions until the condition ends. 

Additionally, you emanate a nimbus of light. You shed [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2402) in a 30-foot radius (and [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2403) to the next 30 feet). You can suppress or reestablish this light as a single action that has the concentrate trait. As long as your nimbus is active, all allies in the area of your nimbus gain a +1 status bonus to saves against fear. You gain the Bless Ally action.

**Bless Ally** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612), [mythic](/Traits.aspx?ID=835)) **Frequency** once per 10 minutes; **Requirements** Your nimbus is active; **Target** a willing ally within the bright light of your nimbus; **Effect** Your radiance grants an ally some of your celestial grace. That ally can roll twice and use the higher result for the next Will save they attempt before the beginning of your next turn.

<title level="2">Ascended Celestial Dedication leads to...</title>
[A Thousand Cries For Help](/Feats.aspx?ID=7337), [Abjure Harm](/Feats.aspx?ID=7336), [Aegis for the Innocent](/Feats.aspx?ID=7332), [Army of One](/Feats.aspx?ID=7340), [Ascend](/Feats.aspx?ID=7333), [Celestial Armaments](/Feats.aspx?ID=7338), [Celestial Rebirth](/Feats.aspx?ID=7344), [Channel Divine Spark](/Feats.aspx?ID=7335), [Cleansing Spell](/Feats.aspx?ID=7339), [Consult Celestial Advisor](/Feats.aspx?ID=7334), [Eyes Unclouded](/Feats.aspx?ID=7341), [Shining Glory](/Feats.aspx?ID=7342)
name: Ascended Celestial Dedication

markdown:
**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 115 **Archetype** [Ascended Celestial](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=289) **Prerequisites** [Ascended Celestial Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7331) **Requirements** Your nimbus is active. --- You transfer a portion of your divine spark to an ally, temporarily dimming your nimbus to encase an ally in a barrier of light that repels attackers. Select a willing creature within the [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2402) of your nimbus. You surround the target in a protective barrier of light; this is a 10-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) centered on the target. This barrier lasts for 1 minute, or until you [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) it. While the Aegis of the Innocent is active, the area of your nimbus is halved. The target gains a +1 status bonus to AC. Any enemy within the barrier, or entering the barrier, must attempt a Fortitude saving throw against your class DC or your spell DC. A creature needs to attempt this save only once each round. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature becomes [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) for 1 round and treats the space within the barrier as [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366).
**Failure** The creature becomes [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) for 1 minute, is pushed 10 feet, and treats the space within the barrier as difficult terrain. If a creature would be pushed into a solid barrier or another creature, it stops at that point and takes 2d6 bludgeoning damage.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the creature is also [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) for 1 round.
name: Aegis for the Innocent

    pfs=""

[Ascend](/Feats.aspx?ID=7333) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Prerequisites**
[Ascended Celestial Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7331)

</column>

---

As you strive to ascend to the ranks of celestials, the pull of gravity loosens on your body. You gain a [fly Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2350) equal to your land Speed and the Flyby Attack action. When you gain this feat, you can choose to sprout wings, if desired; these wings are permanent. 

Once per day as a single action, you can fly at incredible speeds. When you do, your fly Speed becomes equal to double your Speed for 5 rounds.

**Flyby Attack** <actions string="Two Actions" /> **Effect** You Fly and can make a Strike at any point along the way.
name: Ascend

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 14"
    pfs=""
>
[Consult Celestial Advisor](/Feats.aspx?ID=7334) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Exploration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=595" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 115

**Archetype** [Ascended Celestial](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=289)

**Frequency**
once per day

**Prerequisites**
[Ascended Celestial Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7331)

</column>

---

For many, the Calling of an ascended celestial is a wondrous but challenging path, which they must walk alone. This isn’t the case for you. A single celestial is responsible for shepherding you into the celestial ranks and has offered to serve as a mentor to you throughout the time of your transformation. You can call upon this mentor for advice, guidance, and support. Once per day, you can spend 30 minutes in deep meditation. During this time, you spiritually converse with your celestial advisor. You gain three benefits during this conversation, which can occur in any order of your choosing.

You discuss a topic of interest with your celestial advisor. This allows you to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) on any topic once, with mythic proficiency. You can spend 1 [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320) to instead Recall Knowledge on any three topics with mythic proficiency. 

You discuss a goal, activity, or event with your celestial advisor. This must be a goal you plan to achieve or an event you expect to happen within 1 week. Your celestial advisor gives you a piece of advice to help you achieve your goal or to see your way through the expected event. This has the effect of [_read omens_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1647).

You discuss an immediate action that you intend to take with your celestial advisor. This must be an action you plan to take within the next 8 hours. Your celestial advisor gives you their opinion on the outcome. This has the effect of [_augury_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1445), except that the augury can see 8 hours into the future, and the GM doesn’t roll a flat check for a failure.
name: Consult Celestial Advisor

[Ascended Celestial Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7331)

</column>

---

You embrace your divine spark, unleashing your full potential. Your nimbus automatically activates (if it isn’t already) and the area of your nimbus doubles; you shed [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2402) for 60 feet (and [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2403) to the next 60 feet). You can’t suppress your nimbus while Channeling your Divine Spark. You gain a number of temporary Hit Points equal to your level, and [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) to physical damage equal to half your level. You gain the [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=89) condition and can use the extra action each round only for [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) and [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) actions. Each time that you damage a creature with a melee Strike, you can attempt to [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=2380) or [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=2382) that creature as a free action. These effects last for 1 minute.

<title level="2">Channel Divine Spark leads to...</title>
[Scouring Spark](/Feats.aspx?ID=7343)

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 16"
    pfs=""
>
[Celestial Armaments](/Feats.aspx?ID=7338) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 116

**Prerequisites**
[Ascended Celestial Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7331)

</column>

---

You imbue a fragment of your divine spark into a weapon, blessing your blade and transforming it into a weapon worthy of the celestial realms. Select one weapon or [handwraps of mighty blows](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3086) when you make your [daily preparations](/Rules.aspx?ID=2575). The weapon sheds light like a [torch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2760), deals an additional 1d8 spirit damage, and creatures who wield it gain the weapon’s [critical specialization](/Rules.aspx?ID=2203).

When you critically hit a target with the weapon, the target must succeed at a Fortitude save against your class DC or spell DC (whichever is higher) or be [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) for 1d4 rounds. 

As a single action that has the [concentration](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) trait, you can hold your hand aloft and call this weapon back to your possession; it immediately teleports into your hand.

While you’re holding this weapon, you can spend 1 Mythic Point as a single action to temporarily grant a blessing to weapons wielded by your allies. Each ally within the [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2402) of your nimbus when you take this action selects one weapon in their possession. For 1 minute, the selected weapons gain the following benefits: the weapon sheds light like a torch, the weapon deals an additional 1d6 [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) damage, and creatures who wield the weapon gain the weapon’s critical specialization.
name: Celestial Armaments

    pfs=""
>
[Cleansing Spell](/Feats.aspx?ID=7339) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
<trait label="Spellshape" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=513" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 116

**Prerequisites**
[Ascended Celestial Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7331)

</column>

---

Infused with celestial light, your magic can more effectively heal wounds and purge afflictions. If your next action is to [Cast a Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734) with the [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623) trait on a living creature, one target of that spell regains a number of Hit Points equal to your level (in addition to any Hit Points they would normally gain). In addition, you can attempt to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) a [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578) or [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) affecting one target of that spell. These two targets can be the same creature or different creatures, as long as they are both targets of the healing spell.
name: Cleansing Spell

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 18"
    pfs=""
>
[Army of One](/Feats.aspx?ID=7340) <actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Flourish" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=606" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 116

**Archetype** [Ascended Celestial](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=289)

**Prerequisites**
[Ascended Celestial Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7331)

**Requirements**
Your nimbus is active.

</column>

---

Even alone, you’re a force to be reckoned with— an army of one, so long as you have your celestial armaments. Spend 1 Mythic Point. If you’re not currently wielding your celestial armament, it immediately teleports into your hand. With a gesture and a whispered prayer, you launch your celestial armament into the air, where it flies with a mind of its own, attacking every enemy within your nimbus. Make a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) with that weapon against each enemy in the [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2402) of your nimbus. Each attack counts toward your multiple attack penalty, but your penalty doesn’t increase until you have made all your attacks. After these attacks are complete, your weapon returns to your hand.
name: Army of One

When a creature attempts to Lie to you, they roll the [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) check to Lie twice and use the worse result; this is a [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=654) effect.

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 117 **Archetype** [Ascended Celestial](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=289) **Prerequisites** [Ascended Celestial Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7331) **Requirements** Your nimbus is active. --- With a righteous word, your divine aura inspires your allies to great heroics and causes foes to tremble. Spend 1 Mythic Point. All allies in the [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2402) of your nimbus gain a +3 status bonus to attack rolls and skill checks, and the status bonus they receive to saving throws against fear increases to +3. These bonuses last for 1 minute or as long as they remain in the bright light of your nimbus, whichever comes first. Each enemy in the bright light of your nimbus attempts a Will saving throw against your class DC or spell DC (whichever is higher) with the following effects. For the next minute, an enemy can’t reduce its [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) condition below 1 while it remains in the bright light of your nimbus. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is frightened 1, but can immediately [Step](/Actions.aspx?ID=2304) as long it moves away from you.
**Failure** The creature is frightened 2 and you are concealed to that creature while it has the frightened condition.
**Critical Failure** The creature is frightened 3 and you are [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79) to that creature while it has the frightened condition.
name: Shining Glory

</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

---

When you unleash your divine spark, you become a being of living light that scours away the darkness. While Channeling your Divine Spark, you become immune to the [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) and [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) conditions. Any creature that touches you or damages you with an unarmed melee attack or a non-[reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=684) melee weapon takes 3d6 fire damage, your unarmed attacks deal 1d6 additional fire damage, and your fire spells deal one additional die of fire damage (of the same damage die the spell uses). This damage is doubled against creatures with the [aberration](/Traits.aspx?ID=523), [fiend](/Traits.aspx?ID=600), or [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) trait.
name: Scouring Spark

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 20"
    pfs=""
>
[Celestial Rebirth](/Feats.aspx?ID=7344) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 117

**Archetype** [Ascended Celestial](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=289)

**Prerequisites**
[Ascended Celestial Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7331)

</column>

---

Through your diligence and commitment to heroics, you finally complete your apotheosis into a true [celestial](/Traits.aspx?ID=551). Your appearance changes as desired, becoming a perfect expression of your true self. You cease aging, and you can’t die from old age. You become immune to [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) and [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578). You gain the celestial and [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) traits, as well as the [agathion](/Traits.aspx?ID=349), [angel](/Traits.aspx?ID=530), [archon](/Traits.aspx?ID=536), or [azata](/Traits.aspx?ID=543) trait, depending on whose ranks you are joining. 

You become a vessel of vitality. You can cast [_breath of life_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1456) once per day as a 5th-rank innate [divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2) spell and [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554) twice per day as a 7th-rank innate divine spell. When you [cast a spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734) with the [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623) trait that doesn’t target you, you channel this healing energy through your own body before releasing it. You regain 1d6 Hit Points per level of the spell. In addition, you can cast [_raise dead_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1645) as a 10th-rank innate divine spell. When you do, the cost to cast this spell changes to “spend 1 [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320).”

If you die, you’re immediately reborn as a [nephilim](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=68) of an ancestry of your choosing or decided in conjunction with your GM. You are descended from the celestials whose trait you bear—agathion, angel, archon, or azata. This might be represented by a lineage feat. You can choose if you will be reborn on [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26), [Elysium](/Planes.aspx?ID=40), [Heaven](/Planes.aspx?ID=41), or [Nirvana](/Planes.aspx?ID=44). Regardless of where you’re reborn, celestials watch over you, keeping you safe throughout your life. When you reach the age of maturity, you regain your previous powers and memories, becoming an ascended celestial once again. These memories are faint and dreamlike, as if they happened to another person, giving you perspective and history, but not supplanting your current personality or emotional connections.
name: Celestial Rebirth

    level="1"
    right="Feat 12"
    pfs=""
>
[Beast Lord Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7345) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Destiny" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=838" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 118

**Archetype** [Beast Lord](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=290)

**Prerequisites**
a [mature animal companion](Companions.aspx) or an [advanced construct companion](Rules.aspx?ID=1600)

</column>

---

Your Calling has united your destiny with a [companion](/Traits.aspx?ID=558), harnessing a connection between you that links your minds, hearts, and souls. Choose a companion that qualified you for this feat as your united companion. Your united companion increases its Intelligence modifier to +0 or by 1 if its Intelligence modifier is greater than +0. It gains the ability to understand a language that you know and can communicate with you, but only you can understand your companion when it speaks, as it uses chirps, grunts, or other noises to convey its meaning. If you or your united companion are conscious and within 30 feet of each other and either must attempt a saving throw against a [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) effect, both you and your united companion attempt the saving throw. The target of the effect uses the best result; this also applies if both you and your united companion must attempt a saving throw against the same effect.

<title level="2">Beast Lord Dedication leads to...</title>
[Bat Around](/Feats.aspx?ID=7350), [Chosen Ward](/Feats.aspx?ID=7346), [Creature of Myth](/Feats.aspx?ID=7354), [Defend Our Union](/Feats.aspx?ID=7351), [Guarded Domain](/Feats.aspx?ID=7347), [One Life, Two Vessels](/Feats.aspx?ID=7356), [Pack Of The Beast Lord](/Feats.aspx?ID=7355), [Telepathic Union](/Feats.aspx?ID=7352), [Unified Stance](/Feats.aspx?ID=7353), [We are One](/Feats.aspx?ID=7348), [You Can't Hide From Us](/Feats.aspx?ID=7349)
name: Beast Lord Dedication
prerequisite_markdown: a [mature animal companion](Companions.aspx) or an [advanced construct companion](Rules.aspx?ID=1600)

[Chosen Ward](/Feats.aspx?ID=7346) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

[Beast Lord Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7345)

</column>

---

You have designated an ally to be you and your [companion’s](/Traits.aspx?ID=558) ward, making it your primary directive to shield them from harm. During your daily preparations, choose an ally who isn’t your united companion. While either you or your united companion are adjacent to your ward, you grant them a +1 circumstance bonus to their AC and Reflex saves. 

When your ward attempts a Reflex saving throw while adjacent to either you or your united companion, you can spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320) as a reaction to allow your ward to roll that Reflex save twice and take the better result. This is a [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612) effect.

**Special** If you have the [Defend Our Union](/Feats.aspx?ID=7351)  feat, it can also be triggered when a foe successfully Strikes your ward; if used in this way, the damage to your ward is reduced if your Strike is successful instead.
name: Chosen Ward

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 14"
    pfs=""
>
[Guarded Domain](/Feats.aspx?ID=7347) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 119

**Archetype** [Beast Lord](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=290)

**Prerequisites**
[Beast Lord Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7345)

</column>

---

You and your united [companion](/Traits.aspx?ID=558) have elected to become guardians of a particular location or domain. If you spend 1 week of downtime within a settlement or natural area with a 5-mile radius, you and your companion can become guardians of the area and consider it to be your guarded domain. Your guarded domain remains under your protection until you spend more than 1 month outside of your domain, after which you must spend another week of downtime to re-attune to your guarded domain. You can only have one guarded domain at a time, and selecting a new guarded domain immediately ends your protection over the previous guarded domain. 

If a creature has been in your guarded domain for at least 1 hour while you or your companion are also inside your domain, you or your companion can attempt to [Gather Information](/Actions.aspx?ID=2391) about the target by spending 1 minute communing with the land or its residents. You attempt this check at mythic proficiency.

In addition, once per day as a 3-action activity you can spend a Mythic Point to instantly teleport yourself and your united companion to a location you are aware of within your domain. This is a [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710) effect.
name: Guarded Domain

You and your united companion can share each other’s spaces. When you do so, both you and your united companion provide [lesser cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=2372) to one another. You and your united companion also count as being adjacent to one another when sharing each other’s spaces. A target is automatically [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) against the first [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) each round each of you and your united companion make while sharing one another’s spaces; this benefit also applies if you are [mounted](/Traits.aspx?ID=411) on your united companion.

<traits>
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">
