</column>

---

When you and your [companion](/Traits.aspx?ID=558) attack together, you can ensure that escape is but a whispered dream. [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) the same creature that your united companion hit. If your Strike is successful, the targeted creature has been marked for death by you and your companion and can’t become [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79) or [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) to you and your companion for 1 minute or until it moves more than 60 feet away from either you or your companion, whichever comes first. If the triggering creature is under an effect or spell that would normally grant it this condition, you can immediately attempt to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) the effect. Your counteract rank is half your level rounded up, and your counteract check modifier is equal to the higher of your class DC –10 or spell DC –10.
name: You Can't Hide From Us

As long as your Strike deals damage, you can attempt an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check to either [Reposition](/Actions.aspx?ID=2379) or [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=2380) the creature you hit. Both attacks count toward your multiple attack penalty, but it doesn’t increase until after you’ve made both attacks. If your Reposition or Shove moves the target and it ends this movement within your united companion’s reach, your companion can Strike the target. Unless it critically fails, your companion can also attempt an Athletics check to Reposition or Shove the creature. These attacks affect your united companion’s multiple attack penalty the same way.

You can protect your [companion](/Traits.aspx?ID=558) from danger with a welltimed attack. You can immediately [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) toward the triggering enemy and can make a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) against them if they would be in the first range increment of your ranged weapon or within your melee [reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=684). If this Strike is successful, reduce the damage to your companion by your level, or twice your level if the Strike was a critical success.

[Telepathic Union](/Feats.aspx?ID=7352) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Linguistic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=642" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=647" />

Your union is so strong, you can communicate with each other without uttering a word, even across vast distances. You and your united [companion](/Traits.aspx?ID=558) can communicate telepathically while within 1 mile of each other as if you were speaking your shared language. You and your companion can also sense each other’s general emotions as long as you are on the same plane, allowing you to know if the other is in peril or under great distress.

markdown:
**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 120 **Archetype** [Beast Lord](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=290) **Prerequisites** [Beast Lord Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7345); you have a united companion with a [specialization](/Rules.aspx?ID=2120). --- Your specialized united companion grows with unparalleled power and strength, becoming a creature of myth. It gains one of the following effects.
  • **Baleful Body** Your united companion’s blood is boiling, its mechanical fluid is acidic, or its insides are otherwise destructive to the touch. Choose [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), or [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669). If your united companion would take damage from a melee attack, it deals half your level in damage of the chosen type to the creature that made the melee attack. By spending a Mythic Point when you Command your united companion, your united companion can spew its corrosive power as a 2-action activity to deal 14d6 damage of the chosen type in a 15-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) or 30-foot [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388) ([basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save against your class DC or spell DC, whichever is higher).
  • **Chimeric Heads** Your united companion sprouts an extra head which gives it extra eyes and allows it to make additional attacks with related unarmed attacks. Your united companion gains [all-around vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=49). By spending a Mythic Point when you Command your united companion, your united companion can make two [Strikes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) with its unarmed attacks as a single action, each using its current multiple attack penalty. Both Strikes must have the same target. If both attacks hit, combine their damage, and then add any other applicable effects. This counts as one attack when calculating your united companion’s multiple attack penalty. Your united companion must have a head and at least one unarmed attack that uses its mouth or head (such as a beak, jaws, or mandible attack) to select this.
  • **Energy Aegis** Your united companion is protected from hazardous energy. Your united companion becomes [immune](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) to your choice of acid, [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), or fire. Your companion gains a +1 status bonus to AC and saves that originate from creatures with the listed trait and to effects and spells with the listed trait. By spending a Mythic Point when you Command your united companion, your united companion can take a single action with the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) trait to extend these same benefits to all creatures who are adjacent to it until the beginning of your next turn.
  • **Magnificent Flight** Your united companion grows wings or learns some other method of flight. Your united companion gains a [Fly speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2350) equal to its Speed and it gains the [mount](/Actions.aspx?ID=2314) special ability. If your united companion already had the mount special ability or a Fly speed equal to its Speed, it instead increases its Speed by 10 feet. By spending a Mythic Point when you Command your united companion, your united companion can, as a two-action activity, Fly up to twice its speed and make a single unarmed melee attack at any point along the way.
  • **Protective Skin** Your united companion is hardened by the infused magic of otherworldly powers. Its maximum Hit Points increase by 30 but it gains a [weakness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2317) of 10 to either [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2916) or [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920). By spending a Mythic Point when you Command your united companion, your united companion gains [fast healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=62) equal to your level for 1 minute.
name: Creature of Myth
prerequisite_markdown: [Beast Lord Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7345); you have a united companion with a [specialization](/Rules.aspx?ID=2120).

You and your united [companion](/Traits.aspx?ID=558) call upon the service of others of its kind, creating figment duplicates of your companion to form a massive swarm of formidable power. Spend a Mythic Point. Your united companion becomes Gargantuan for 1 minute. It gains [immunity](/Rules.aspx?ID=2313) to precision damage, [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) to physical damage equal to half your level, and a [weakness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2317) to area damage equal to half your level. While in this form, your united companion gains the Amassed Assault action, which you immediately Command them to use as the final step of this activation. 

**Amassed Assault** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540)) Each enemy in your united companion's space takes damage equal to one of its unarmed Strikes plus your level ([basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save against your class DC or spell DC, whichever is higher).

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 20"
    pfs=""
>
[One Life, Two Vessels](/Feats.aspx?ID=7356) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 121

**Archetype** [Beast Lord](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=290)

**Prerequisites**
[Beast Lord Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7345)

</column>

---

The minds of you and your [companion](/Traits.aspx?ID=558) are so intertwined that, so long as one of you lives, the other continues to exist in the living one’s mind. If your united companion ever dies or is [destroyed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=46) and it is within 30 feet of you, its consciousness is drawn into your mind, where it can communicate with you normally and remains there until your next [daily preparations](/Rules.aspx?ID=2575). During your next daily preparations, you can call upon your union to restore your companion to life again in a ritual that takes 1 hour. Your restored united companion returns whole-bodied, at their maximum Hit Point total, and cured of any afflictions that may have been afflicting it when it died.

If you would ever die or be destroyed and you are within 30 feet of your united companion, you consciousness flows into your companion for 3 days. During this time, you can perform actions as if you are your united companion using its abilities and characteristics for any actions you perform. After spending 3 days in this state, you are returned to life with a number of Hit Points equal to twice your level and regain a Mythic Point.

If you or your united companion die while sharing your body with the other’s consciousness, you truly die, but if you are not returned to life within 1 year, you are both reincarnated as children (or in a similarly immature state) in a location near where you first became companions. Neither you nor your companion complete your journeys along the River of Souls, nor do you possess any knowledge of the other’s reincarnated form. However, you both feel an inkling of your former connection, should you ever meet. If either of you are killed before you are together again as companions, the cycle of reincarnation is broken, and you can both die as normal.
name: One Life, Two Vessels

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 12"
    pfs=""
>
[Broken Chain Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7357) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Destiny" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=838" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 123

**Archetype** [Broken Chain](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=291)

</column>

---

Your Calling was sparked by your outrage at tyrants, after having witnessed a brutal act of oppression that you could no longer abide. You may spend a Mythic Point when you attempt an [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) or [Force Open](/Actions.aspx?ID=2375) check to do so at mythic proficiency. Additionally, you gain the Ultimatum of Liberation action (see below), but you also gain the anathema, “You must not use Ultimatum of Liberation on a creature you don’t believe to be oppressing others or who has conceded their power”. If you break this anathema, you lose access to abilities granted by this archetype until you atone or make amends. 

Your GM has final say about what constitutes oppression, which can be a difficult and sometimes nebulous concept. In general, oppression is the cruel and unjust use of authority or power. Some examples include performing acts of violence against those without power, removing an individual’s agency through mind control magic, and engaging in organized mistreatment or exploitation of others. When the members of a royal guard assault a beggar just to get him off the street, that is oppression. When a tax collector levies higher charges against the less fortunate because it’s the law, they are participating in a system of oppression.

**Ultimatum of Liberation** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [mythic](/Traits.aspx?ID=835)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You proclaim an ultimatum against a creature you believe is oppressing others and all who follow their orders. All other creatures (including you) who hear your ultimatum, or learn about it later on, are emboldened by your words for 1 day, and gain a +2 status bonus to their Will saving throws against effects produced by the oppressing creature and their allies, which increases to +3 if they are within 30 feet of you. Additionally, the first time each day that creature and each of its allies comes within 30 feet of you, they must succeed on a Will save against your class DC or spell DC (whichever is higher) or become [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) (frightened 2 on a critical failure). This second effect has the [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [_fear_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1524), and [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) traits. The oppressing creature may end all the effects as a free action by truthfully agreeing to permanently end their oppression. Otherwise, your Ultimatum of Liberation has no duration, though you can have only one Ultimatum of Liberation active at a time. If you pronounce a second Ultimatum of Liberation, the effects of the first one end.

<title level="2">Broken Chain Dedication leads to...</title>
[Bloc Tactics](/Feats.aspx?ID=7358), [Break the Causal Chain](/Feats.aspx?ID=7372), [Break the Cycle](/Feats.aspx?ID=7359), [Bring into the Fold](/Feats.aspx?ID=7364), [Cry of Rebellion](/Feats.aspx?ID=7365), [Liberty or Death](/Feats.aspx?ID=7369), [Lose Your Chains](/Feats.aspx?ID=7366), [Rebellious Existence](/Feats.aspx?ID=7370), [Release Me!](/Feats.aspx?ID=7360), [Remember thy Names](/Feats.aspx?ID=7367), [Reprisal of the Fallen](/Feats.aspx?ID=7371), [Rites of Liberation](/Feats.aspx?ID=7361), [Stand Firm](/Feats.aspx?ID=7368), [United Front](/Feats.aspx?ID=7362), [You Can't Keep Us Down](/Feats.aspx?ID=7363), [You Can't Kill an Idea](/Feats.aspx?ID=7373)
name: Broken Chain Dedication

<traits>

---

You have experience in undertaking direct action and know how to protect the identity and health of you and your allies when doing so. During your [daily preparations](/Rules.aspx?ID=2575), you can select a number of targets equal to half your level. You disguise the targets to hide their identities, which gives them a +4 status bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) checks to prevent others from seeing through their disguises, and lets them add their level even if untrained. Additionally, the disguises also protect from smoke, fumes, and inhaled substances, giving the targets a +3 status bonus to saving throws against them. The targets can quickly doff or don their disguises with a two-action activity.
name: Bloc Tactics

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Flourish" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=606" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

**Requirements**
An enemy has you [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90).

</column>

---

Your retribution against one who would detain you is swift and dangerous. Make a melee [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) against the creature who has you [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90). You can still make this attack even if you are restrained, though not if you are wielding a two-handed weapon. Unless your Strike is a critical failure, you can then attempt a check to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296). If the creature who has you grabbed or restrained is the target of your [Ultimatum of Liberation](/Actions.aspx?ID=2993), you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to this check. Both the Strike and the Escape count toward your multiple attack penalty, but it doesn’t increase until you’ve made both rolls.
name: Release Me!

If you would critically fail the ritual, you fail instead. Additionally, you gain the [_unimpeded stride_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1806) and [_word of freedom_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1807) domain spells. These are focus spells, granting you a focus pool of 2 Focus Points or expanding that pool if you already have one. You can Refocus while you meditate on the concepts of freedom and liberty. (For more on focus spells, see [here](/Rules.aspx?ID=2228).)

[United Front](/Feats.aspx?ID=7362) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 124

**Prerequisites**
[Broken Chain Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7357)

</column>

---

Thanks to your powerful speeches, your movement finds allies in all sectors of society and even across enemy lines, all eager to help you accomplish your goals. If you spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320) when you use [Ultimatum of Liberation](/Feats.aspx?ID=7357), you can ask four questions related to that creature, as if you succeeded at four [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) checks. This information comes to you over the course of the next 1 to 2 hours, representing tidbits of information you glean from local supporters. After receiving this information, the first time you roll [Initiative](/Rules.aspx?ID=3300) in an encounter with the target of your Ultimatum of Liberation or their allies, you and all your allies can make your Initiative check with mythic proficiency.
name: United Front

<traits>

<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />

Seeing your allies falter, you fill their hearts with your unbreakable hope, rousing them to stay in the fight. All allies within 60 feet that are [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=69), or [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) and [wounded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=99), are healed a number of Hit Points equal to three times your level. Then, all allies within 60 feet that are [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88) may stand up as a free action that does not provoke reactions.

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 124 **Archetype** [Broken Chain](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=291) **Frequency** once per day **Prerequisites** [Broken Chain Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7357) --- Not all who oppress are irredeemable, and given encouragement and some time, they will abandon their cause and join your fight against the very system they used to uphold. If your next action is to issue an [Ultimatum of Liberation](/Actions.aspx?ID=2993), you may have up to 10 targets who can hear you attempt a Will save against your class DC or spell DC, whichever is higher. You can cause any changes in attitude to last longer than the current social interaction by spending at least 10 minutes speaking with the affected targets earnestly about your cause. **Success** The target is unaffected.
**Failure** The target’s attitude toward you improves by one step.
**Critical Failure** The target’s attitude toward you improves by two steps.
name: Bring into the Fold

This sonic damage increases by 1d4 at 17th level and every level thereafter.

[Remember thy Names](/Feats.aspx?ID=7367) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Auditory" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=541" />
<trait label="Linguistic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=642" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=647" />

</column>

---

You peer into the eyes of a target of your [Ultimatum of Liberation](/Actions.aspx?ID=2993), as you say aloud the names of their victims, searing them into the oppressor’s mind, cursing them to feel their victims’ pain. The target takes 12d6 mental damage ([basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Will save against your class DC or spell DC, whichever is higher). If the target fails or critically fails this check, they also take 2d6 [persistent mental damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86). For the next hour, when the target stops taking this persistent damage, you or one of your allies can mention a victim’s name as a single action to the target, forcing them to succeed at another Will save or begin taking the persistent damage again. This is a [curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566) effect. Once you use Remember Their Names on a target, the target is immune to it for 24 hours.
name: Remember thy Names

[Stand Firm](/Feats.aspx?ID=7368) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Auditory" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=541" />
<trait label="Linguistic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=642" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 124

**Archetype** [Broken Chain](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=291)

**Prerequisites**
[Broken Chain Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7357)

</column>

---

Your words help your ally shake off unease and terror. You shout a bolstering comment at an ally within 30 feet of you who has the [fleeing](/Conditions.aspx?ID=74) or [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) condition and can hear you. That ally reduces the value of their frightened condition by 2 (to a minimum of 0). If they are fleeing, they can immediately attempt a new saving throw against the effect that caused the condition. If that save is a failure, but not a critical failure, the ally needs to spend one fewer action on their turn to escape the source of the fleeing condition. This allows that ally to [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) or [Cast a one-action Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734) that doesn’t necessarily get them further away from the source, but the ally still has the fleeing condition and can’t [Delay](/Actions.aspx?ID=2294) or [Ready](/Actions.aspx?ID=2299) as usual. If the ally is under an effect that reduces the number of actions they have on their turn, they don’t gain this benefit on a failed saving throw. Once you use Stand Firm on an ally, that ally is immune to it until the beginning of your next turn.
name: Stand Firm

<trait label="Incapacitation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=631" />

Spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320) and make a Strike at mythic proficiency against the required creature. If you succeed and deal damage, the target must succeed on a Fortitude save against your class DC or spell DC (whichever is higher) or die. If the target succeeds, they are immune to this ability for 24 hours.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 18"
    pfs=""
>
[Rebellious Existence](/Feats.aspx?ID=7370) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 125

**Archetype** [Broken Chain](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=291)

**Prerequisites**
[Broken Chain Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7357)

</column>

---

You have tied yourself so absolutely to the cause of liberty that your very existence is an act of rebellion. You ignore any [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) effects without the [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598) trait that would rob you of your agency (for example, on a failed save against [_charm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1463) you would still be able to take hostile actions, and against [_laughing fit_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1583) you would still be able to take reactions but would remain [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92), but on a critical failure against fear you would still be fleeing because it’s a fear effect). Additionally, you are [immune](/Rules.aspx?ID=1609) to effects that would imprison your soul (such as [_seize soul_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1664), or a [final blade](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1094)).
name: Rebellious Existence

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 20"
    pfs=""
>
[Break the Causal Chain](/Feats.aspx?ID=7372) <actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 125

**Archetype** [Broken Chain](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=291)

**Frequency**
once per month

**Prerequisites**
[Broken Chain Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7357)

</column>

---

Something terrible has happened. A beloved ally has fallen, a last stand has failed, or a tyrant has beaten you to a crucial goal. Whatever it is, your heart sinks. How can this be? No, this can’t be. You spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320) to alter reality as if you got a success (but not a critical success) with the [wish](/Rituals.aspx?ID=125) ritual. You must choose to completely undo the effects of a past action or set of actions that occurred within the past week to lead to the undesirable outcome. This will bring serious consequences back to haunt you some day. Altering the rhythm of causality is likely to draw the attention of certain [aeons](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=357). [Fey norns](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3108) won’t be too happy with you fraying their precious threads of fate either. Deities whose domains include [change](/Domains.aspx?ID=40), [fate](/Domains.aspx?ID=75), [freedom](/Domains.aspx?ID=77), [time](/Domains.aspx?ID=54), or [tyranny](/Domains.aspx?ID=96) are likely to start keeping tabs on you, if they haven’t already, and send their agents to meddle in your affairs.
name: Break the Causal Chain

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 20"
    pfs=""
>
[You Can't Kill an Idea](/Feats.aspx?ID=7373) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 125

**Archetype** [Broken Chain](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=291)

**Prerequisites**
[Broken Chain Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7357)

</column>

---

Those who would destroy you instead transform you into a powerful yet formless entity. If you would be killed while you have an [Ultimatum of Liberation](/Actions.aspx?ID=2993) active, you (along with your equipment) become an ideaform.

As an ideaform, you are [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=83) and [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=632). You have only 1 Hit Point and can’t gain more, but you are [immune](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) to all damage; if your [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=69) or [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67) condition would increase to a high enough value to kill you, it does not increase, and you are immune to any effect that would instantly kill or destroy you. You are immune to the [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) condition, yet can take only purely [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) actions. You can communicate telepathically with your allies at unlimited range, and gain two extra reactions at the start of each of your turns. Additionally, you can [Aid](/Actions.aspx?ID=2292) allies through nothing but telepathic advice, without having to spend an action to prepare first, and can spend a Mythic Point to do so at mythic proficiency. Any circumstance bonus you grant through Aiding is increased by 1.

Before an hour passes while being an ideaform, you must choose a willing follower of your cause to fuse with and cease to be an ideaform. Use your level, their [ancestry](/Rules.aspx?ID=3286), [heritage](/Rules.aspx?ID=2083), and [ancestry feats](/Rules.aspx?ID=2084), and work with your GM to choose which combination of class, attribute modifiers, skills, and feats would make a good representation of this fusion. This new character has all your previous equipment. If you do not fuse with a follower after an hour of being an ideaform, you cease to be an ideaform and die; the equipment you had at the time of your original death also ceases to exist.
name: You Can't Kill an Idea

    pfs=""
>
[Eternal Legend Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7374) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Destiny" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=838" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 127

**Archetype** [Eternal Legend](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=292)

</column>

---

As your renown as a fighter grows, your Calling comes to fruition. Almost nothing can keep you down. During your [daily preparations](/Rules.aspx?ID=2575), choose [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), or [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71). For the remainder of the day, when you roll a success against an effect that would impose that condition on you, you get a critical success instead. In addition, anytime you gain the chosen condition, reduce its value by 1. You can change the chosen condition with a single action that has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) trait.

<title level="2">Eternal Legend Dedication leads to...</title>
[Death from Above](/Feats.aspx?ID=7380), [Demolishing Knockback](/Feats.aspx?ID=7375), [Earth to Heavens Strike](/Feats.aspx?ID=7376), [Exaltation Overwhelming](/Feats.aspx?ID=7385), [Fautless Defense](/Feats.aspx?ID=7377), [Fight Through Oblivion](/Feats.aspx?ID=7389), [Finish Their Story!](/Feats.aspx?ID=7390), [Immovable Object](/Feats.aspx?ID=7378), [Leaping Hedgehog Strike](/Feats.aspx?ID=7381), [Legend of Combat](/Feats.aspx?ID=7386), [Living Epic](/Feats.aspx?ID=7391), [Mythic Weapon Specialization](/Feats.aspx?ID=7387), [Pierce The Eye](/Feats.aspx?ID=7382), [Scattering Shout](/Feats.aspx?ID=7379), [Terrifying Mien](/Feats.aspx?ID=7383), [Weft and Warp](/Feats.aspx?ID=7384)
name: Eternal Legend Dedication

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 14"
    pfs=""
>
[Demolishing Knockback](/Feats.aspx?ID=7375) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Flourish" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=606" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 127

**Archetype** [Eternal Legend](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=292)

**Prerequisites**
[Eternal Legend Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7374)

</column>

---

You make a forceful attack that has the power to push your foe through walls. Spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320) and make a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) at mythic proficiency. If you hit and deal damage to the target, you push them up to 15 feet away from you. If this movement would cause the target to collide with an object that has a [hardness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2183) of 5 or less, the object [breaks](/Conditions.aspx?ID=60) and the target is pushed through the object’s space for the remainder of the [forced movement](/Rules.aspx?ID=2364), taking an additional 2d6 bludgeoning damage (the GM can decide that this is a different type of damage depending on the object the target was pushed through; for instance, slashing damage is the target is pushed through a pane of glass). If the forced movement doesn’t cause the target to collide with an object, the target must succeed at a Reflex saving throw against your class DC or be knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88) at the end of the movement.
name: Demolishing Knockback

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 14"
    pfs=""
>
[Earth to Heavens Strike](/Feats.aspx?ID=7376) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 127

**Archetype** [Eternal Legend](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=292)

**Prerequisites**
[Eternal Legend Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7374)

</column>

---

Your blows hamper both a foe’s mobility and cognitive functions by focusing first on their lower extremities and then their head. Make a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306); if you hit and deal damage, the creature is also [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1 until the end of your next turn. Then, make another Strike against the same creature. If that Strike hits and deals damage, the creature is also [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 until the end of your next turn. If both Strikes hit and deal damage, the duration of both conditions increases to 1 minute. Each Strike counts toward your multiple attack penalty, but you don’t increase your multiple attack penalty until after you’ve made both Strikes.
name: Earth to Heavens Strike

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 16"
    pfs=""
>
[Death from Above](/Feats.aspx?ID=7380) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 128

**Archetype** [Eternal Legend](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=292)

**Prerequisites**
[Eternal Legend Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7374)

**Requirements**
You are adjacent to a climbable surface at least 10 feet tall.

</column>

---

Seemingly to defy gravity, you use a nearby vertical surface to propel yourself upward and then [strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) downward like the driving rain. [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) up to half your Speed upward on the required surface; if you have a [climb Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2349), you may use your full climb Speed instead. The surface does not need to span the full height of your Stride. You can then either make a ranged Strike at a target within your weapon’s first range increment and land in the space you began (or an adjacent space) without taking [falling](/Rules.aspx?ID=2352) damage, or you can land in a space adjacent to a creature within 10 feet of the required surface without taking falling damage and make a melee Strike against that creature. Either Strike deals an extra two dice of weapon damage. In addition, the target dealt damage by your Strike must succeed at a Fortitude saving throw against your class DC or become [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) 1.
name: Death from Above
requirement_markdown: You are adjacent to a climbable surface at least 10 feet tall.

[Leaping Hedgehog Strike](/Feats.aspx?ID=7381) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

</column>

---

You pounce toward an enemy and then barrel them over with one fluid movement, ending this maneuver with an attack that may catch them off-balance. [Leap](/Actions.aspx?ID=2298) horizontally toward an enemy you can see; treat your Speed as 30 feet or more for this Leap if it is less than 30 feet. If you end this movement adjacent to that enemy, attempt an [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) check to [Tumble Through](/Actions.aspx?ID=2370) that enemy’s space. On a success, the target is also knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88). You then Strike that same enemy. If the target has been knocked prone, you can spend 1 [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320) as part of this Strike to make it at mythic proficiency.
name: Leaping Hedgehog Strike

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 128 **Archetype** [Eternal Legend](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=292) **Prerequisites** [Eternal Legend Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7374) **Requirements** You are wielding a ranged weapon. --- You aim a shot from your weapon directly at a foe’s eye, blinding them in the process. Make a ranged [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306). On a hit, the target additionally takes 2d8 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86). The target must also attempt a Fortitude save against your class DC with the following effects. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) until the end of its next turn.
**Failure** The target is blinded for as long as they are taking the persistent bleed damage and for 1 additional round thereafter.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the target also takes a –2 circumstance penalty to the [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333) to remove the persistent bleed damage; a creature [Administering First Aid](/Actions.aspx?ID=2396) to stop this persistent bleed damage takes a –2 circumstance penalty to the [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) check.
name: Pierce The Eye
requirement_markdown: You are wielding a ranged weapon.

[Terrifying Mien](/Feats.aspx?ID=7383) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Prerequisites**
[Eternal Legend Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7374); Master in [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40)

</column>

---

Whether they have heard of your legend or seen your prowess on the field of battle, those already quaking in their boots grow weak when forced to stand against you. You gain [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) against bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage against [Strikes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) made by creatures with the [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) condition. This resistance is equal to four times the value of that creature’s frightened condition. When this resistance applies to at least one of a creature’s Strikes during their turn, that creature doesn’t reduce the value of their frightened condition at the end of their turn.
name: Terrifying Mien

[Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) up to half your speed toward an enemy; if you end this movement adjacent to them make a melee [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) against that foe. If you hit, you can [Reposition](/Actions.aspx?ID=2379) the target into your square at the same time that you [Step](/Actions.aspx?ID=2304) into their square. You then make another Strike against the same target, who is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) to the attack. Finally, you can Step away from that enemy.

You fulminate with power and glory, making it clear to all who see you that your legend is real and possibly even greater than purported. Spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320).  All enemies who can see you must succeed at a Will saving throw against your class DC or become [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 3 (frightened 4 on a critical failure). You and any ally who can see you may choose to [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) or [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) as a free action. Any of these Strikes are made at mythic proficiency.

</summary>
summary_markdown: When you fully embrace your Calling, your blows hit with incredible force that can fell even the mightiest of creatures.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 18"
    pfs=""
>
[Unstoppable Force](/Feats.aspx?ID=7388) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 129

**Archetype** [Eternal Legend](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=292)

**Prerequisites**
[Immovable Object](/Feats.aspx?ID=7378)

</column>

---

While you are in the stance provided by Immovable Object, you ignore [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) and greater difficult terrain. Once per 10 minutes, if you [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) your Immovable Object stance, you can Stride as a free action. At the end of that Stride, your final footfall causes an explosion of energy. Each creature within a 20-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) takes 14d6 force damage (basic Reflex save against your class DC). Creatures that fail the save are also knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88).
name: Unstoppable Force

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 20"
    pfs=""
>
[Fight Through Oblivion](/Feats.aspx?ID=7389) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 129

**Archetype** [Eternal Legend](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=292)

**Prerequisites**
[Eternal Legend Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7374)

</column>

---

Mortal wounds that would kill other warriors are no more than the flutter of butterfly wings to you. When you are reduced to 0 Hit Points, you aren’t knocked [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) and you don’t gain the dying condition. Until the start of your next turn, you become [immune](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) to all damage, but you also can’t regain Hit Points in any way except the following. If you Strike and damage a foe on this turn, you regain 4d8+30 Hit Points. Otherwise, your [wounded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=99) value increases by 1 and you remain immune to damage and [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623). This cycle continues until you regain Hit Points by damaging a foe or until you reach wounded 4, at which point you lose the wounded condition, fall unconscious, and gain the [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=69) condition.
name: Fight Through Oblivion

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 20"
    pfs=""
>
[Finish Their Story!](/Feats.aspx?ID=7390) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 129

**Archetype** [Eternal Legend](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=292)

**Prerequisites**
[Eternal Legend Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7374)

</column>

---

Though your tale is immortal, your foes aren’t afforded such an advantage and mainly serve only to further your legend. When the time is right, you can shepherd a weaker opponent off this mortal coil. Spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320) and make a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) at mythic proficiency against an enemy. This Strike counts as two attacks for your multiple attack penalty and either kills or damages the target depending on their level, as noted below. After you make this Strike, the target becomes temporarily immune to Finish Their Story!, for 1 hour.

**16th Level or Lower** If the Strike hits, the target dies instantly.

**17th Level** If the Strike hits, the target takes the Strike’s normal damage plus an additional three damage die.

**18th Level or Higher** If the Strike hits, the target takes the Strike’s normal damage plus an additional two damage die.
name: Finish Their Story!

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 20"
    pfs=""
>
[Living Epic](/Feats.aspx?ID=7391) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 129

**Archetype** [Eternal Legend](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=292)

**Prerequisites**
[Eternal Legend Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7374)

</column>

---

You are a living legend, a being who cannot be claimed by [death](/Rules.aspx?ID=2329), as death has already passed you. You are beyond whatever they wish to make you. When you would be killed, you instead disappear. You reappear anywhere where your name is spoken in the next week. Your name must be said in the context of recounting one of your exploits, including your death. If the person speaking your name is a close ally (such as another PC), you return to life with only 1 Hit Point. However, if a stranger speaks your name, you return to life with full Hit Points and you gain a +1 status bonus to attack rolls, Perception, saving throws, and skill checks for 1 week. If no one speaks your name within a week of your death, your soul enters the River of Souls, and you can be brought back to life using other means.
name: Living Epic

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 12"
    pfs=""
>
[Godling Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7392) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Destiny" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=838" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 131

**Archetype** [Godling](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=293)

</column>

---

Your Calling allows you to begin walking the path to true deific ascension, inspiring followers as your divine seed grows. Your first follower is your hierophant—the high priest of your new faith—whom you rely on to spread your word and provide an anchoring mortal presence as your perspectives are increasingly pulled toward the cosmic. When you gain this feat, choose one willing ally to be your hierophant. If your hierophant is not trained in spell attack modifier and spell DC, they become trained for as long as they are your designated hierophant. If your hierophant dies or chooses to part ways with you, you can designate a new hierophant by communing with the chosen ally for a period of 24 hours. 

Your Calling allows you to seize the [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579) magic typically restricted to deities. Choose one domain to claim (see list of domains [here](Domains.aspx)). You learn the initial domain spell for this domain. You gain a pool of [Focus](/Rules.aspx?ID=285) Points with 1 Focus Point and you can Refocus by taking 10 minutes to replenish your divine energy from the planes beyond. You gain expert proficiency in spell modifier and spell DC, and your spellcasting ability for these spells is your choice of Wisdom or Charisma, decided when you gain this feat.

Your hierophant also gains the [Cast a Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734) activity and can cast the domain spell you learned with Godling Dedication. When they Cast the Spell in this way, your Focus Point is expended as the cost of the spell. Your hierophant uses their own spellcasting proficiency in spell modifier and spell DC when casting a spell in this way, and they use their own Wisdom or Charisma modifier (matching your choice). If your hierophant has mythic power, they can spend a Mythic Point to cast any divine spell they gain by being your hierophant at mythic proficiency.

<title level="2">Godling Dedication leads to...</title>
[Absolve Sins](/Feats.aspx?ID=7396), [Assume Godhood](/Feats.aspx?ID=7402), [Claim Cardinal Domains](/Feats.aspx?ID=7393), [Divine Invulnerability](/Feats.aspx?ID=7394), [Font of Life or Death](/Feats.aspx?ID=7397), [Gird Champion](/Feats.aspx?ID=7398), [Heroic Weakness](/Feats.aspx?ID=8014), [Myth of Quick-Thinking](/Feats.aspx?ID=8015), [Myth of Realm-Walking](/Feats.aspx?ID=8016), [Pass Vengeful Judgement](/Feats.aspx?ID=7399), [Receive Prayers](/Feats.aspx?ID=7395), [Sanctified Relic](/Feats.aspx?ID=7400), [Stymie the Gods](/Feats.aspx?ID=7401)
name: Godling Dedication

</traits>

<additional-info>

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 14"
    pfs=""
>
[Claim Cardinal Domains](/Feats.aspx?ID=7393) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 131

**Archetype** [Godling](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=293)

**Prerequisites**
[Godling Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7392)

</column>

---

Your dominion over the divine truths of the world increases. You can lay claim to up to three additional domains, to a total maximum of four. Your mortal form cannot easily contain such power, however—each day during your daily preparations, decide which of your domains you are currently drawing power from; you gain access to this domain for the day and can cast its domain spells as normal, while your ability to access the power of your other domains lies dormant. You learn the advanced domain spell for any domain that you have claimed with your godling abilities.

When you gain this feat, you can choose not to lay claim to one or more domains immediately, instead leaving the space open until later in your story. As long as you have one or more domains unclaimed, you can spend a Mythic Point as a single action to claim a domain, even in the heat of battle, immediately making this your active domain. This domain remains a part of your portfolio forever and cannot be changed after this point.

You can also spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320) to overcharge your divine power, gaining access to all the domains you have claimed for the next minute and passing this access on to your hierophant as normal. After the minute ends, you can choose which of the four domains you retain access to until your next [daily preparations](/Rules.aspx?ID=2575).
name: Claim Cardinal Domains

You gain [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) to all damage equal to half your level for 1 minute as your aura smolders with power. During this time, if you cast a domain spell you received from this destiny, this resistance increases to equal your level until the beginning of your next turn.

[Receive Prayers](/Feats.aspx?ID=7395) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=647" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

**Archetype** [Godling](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=293)

**Prerequisites**
[Godling Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7392)

</column>

---

You open your senses so the prayers of mortals can better reach your ears. You gain a +1 status bonus to checks to [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=2302), but only when attempting to ascertain hopes, prayers, wishes, and other strongly held desires of the target. You gain an even greater sense of your hierophant's prayers, which grants you a constant [_status_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1690) as a constant [divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2) spell targeting your hierophant. The spell has unlimited duration and functions at any range, included across planar boundaries, and [counteracting](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) the spell merely suppresses it until your next [daily preparations](/Rules.aspx?ID=2575), though it automatically ends if the creature stops being your hierophant. 

You gain the ability to cast [_breath of life_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1456) once per day as a 5th-rank divine innate spell. You can cast this spell only on your hierophant, and the spell has unlimited range (your status spell typically alerting to you to when your hierophant would die).
name: Receive Prayers

<traits>

Your forgiveness is without limit, though the path to penitence can be painful, especially against those who have trespassed against your current mortal form. Spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320); each enemy within a 30-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) takes 14d6 mental damage ([basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Will save against your class DC or spell DC, whichever is higher) as you force them to reflect upon their sins and misdeeds. Any creature who dealt damage to you or your hierophant since the end of your previous turn uses the outcome one degree of success worse than the result of their saving throw.

</title>

<traits>

<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />

---

Your will manifests into divine weapons to protect and arm your followers. When you gain this feat, choose one type of weapon with special significance to you, such as a [longsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=386) or a [sickle](/Weapons.aspx?ID=364). This becomes your favored weapon. You and your hierophant both treat this weapon as a simple weapon for the purposes of proficiency. You and your hierophant gain the [critical specialization](/Rules.aspx?ID=2203) effect of your favored weapon and deal an additional 1d6 spirit damage with [Strikes](/Rules.aspx?ID=2895) made with your favored weapon. If one of your current domain spells can deal a type of damage other than [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), you can choose to match this damage type (for instance, wreathing your weapon in sacred ashes that deal fire damage if you have the [fire](/Domains.aspx?ID=76) domain).
name: Gird Champion

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 18"
    pfs=""
>
[Pass Vengeful Judgement](/Feats.aspx?ID=7399) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
<trait label="Sanctified" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=519" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 132

**Archetype** [Godling](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=293)

**Frequency**
once per day

**Prerequisites**
[Godling Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7392)

**Requirements**
Your hierophant invoked your judgment on their previous turn

</column>

---

To prevent yourself from frivolously calling down the terrible power of divine wrath, you have vowed to do so only with the assent of your mortal hierophant. If your hierophant successfully Strikes a target, they can spend a single action to invoke your wrath. On your turn, you can then Pass Vengeful Judgment on the target, calling down a scouring pillar of divine energy that deals 20d10 [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) damage with a basic Fortitude save. You do not need to have line of effect to the target and it functions at any range. If one of your current domain spells can deal a type of damage other than spirit, you can choose to match this damage type (for instance, calling down a flaming pillar that deals [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) damage if you have the [fire](/Domains.aspx?ID=76) domain).

If you spend a Mythic Point as part of Passing your Vengeful Judgment, the blast extends into the heavens, scattering the clouds in a detonation visible to all creatures within a 10-mile radius as a show of wrath and a warning to those who would dare harm your followers or question your commandments. For the next month, you and your hierophant gain a +2 status bonus to [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) checks against creatures in the area.

**Special** If you have [Font of Life or Death](/Feats.aspx?ID=7397) , you can choose for the damage type to be [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509) (if you chose heal) or [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) (if you chose harm) when you use this ability.
name: Pass Vengeful Judgement
requirement_markdown: Your hierophant invoked your judgment on their previous turn

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 18"
    pfs=""
>
[Sanctified Relic](/Feats.aspx?ID=7400) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 132

**Archetype** [Godling](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=293)

**Prerequisites**
[Godling Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7392)

</column>

---

You fill an item with your divine power, granting the wearer your blessings. When you take this feat, choose two attributes (one of which must be your key attribute) as your divine attributes and either [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) or [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) sanctification; if you have the holy or unholy trait through a class feature or other ability, you must choose that same trait. As part of your [daily preparations](/Rules.aspx?ID=2575), you can touch one worn magical item that doesn’t have the [apex](/Traits.aspx?ID=532) trait. It gains the apex trait until your next daily preparations, and increases the modifier of one of your divine attributes (chosen during your preparations) by 1 or to a total of +4, whichever is higher. In addition, the item grants the wearer the holy or unholy trait as chosen by you. A character can benefit from only one apex item at a time, as normal. You can’t wear this apex item, but your hierophant can. 

If you spend 1 Mythic Point when you create this apex item, it increases the wearer’s modifiers of both of your divine attributes by 1 or to a total of +4, whichever is higher. This is an exception to the usual limit for apex items.
name: Sanctified Relic

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 18"
    pfs=""
>
[Stymie the Gods](/Feats.aspx?ID=7401) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Aura" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=542" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 133

**Archetype** [Godling](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=293)

**Frequency**
once per hour

**Prerequisites**
[Godling Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7392)

</column>

---

By exerting your will as a nascent god present in the mortal realms, you can hold off the influences of most distant entities, even ones much older and more powerful than yourself. Either you or your hierophant are able to use this action. When either of you do, you both gain a +2 status bonus to Armor Class and saving throws against [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579) spells, as well as the spells, [Strikes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306), and abilities of extraplanar creatures, for 3 rounds. This benefit affects both you and your hierophant even if you are on separate planes of existence when this ability is used.

If a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320) is spent by the individual using Stymie the Gods, the bonus increases to +4 and the effects last for 10 minutes.
name: Stymie the Gods

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 20"
    pfs=""
>
[Assume Godhood](/Feats.aspx?ID=7402) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 133

**Archetype** [Godling](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=293)

**Prerequisites**
[Godling Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7392)

</column>

---

You complete your apotheosis into living godhood, allowing you to empower more followers. You can serve as the [deity](/Rules.aspx?ID=2048) for classes with a deity feature, such as clerics, empowering them to cast divine spells with their class features. When you serve as the deity in this way, you can provide any aspect of a deity stat block (such as domains or a favored weapon) that you yourself have acquired from a godling feat. In addition, you can choose one skill you are legendary in to act as your divine skill, and your divine attributes include your key attribute and one other attribute of your choosing.

When you Assume Godhood, choose three spells from any list other than the [divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2) list; one must be 1st rank, one must be 2nd or 3rd rank, and one can be of any rank. These spells become additional cleric spells you can grant to your followers. You gain the Intercession Spell ability, allowing you to directly provide the magic that your hierophant uses to enact your miracles.  

**Intercession Spell** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [spellshape](/Traits.aspx?ID=513)) **Frequency** once per day **Trigger** Your hierophant Casts a Spell; **Effect** Interceding directly in your hierophant's affairs, you grant them additional magic. They do not expend the spell slot, usage, or Focus Point.

If you die after Assuming Godhood, only your mortal body perishes, and you continue your existence as a deity, watching over the Universe from afar. In this deific form, you cannot move, act, or speak normally, though you still grant spells to your followers and can perceive and affect them using your godling abilities like Receive Prayers or Intercession Spell, regardless of range. If you need to take more direct action in creation, you can descend to the Universe and incarnate yourself into a mortal body of your choosing within 1 mile of one of your followers or a holy site relevant to your faith, which has the same statistics and abilities that you had before you died. This new body can have any appearance you wish, but typically retains at least one feature important to your divine iconography, such as eye color, a scar, or a unique birthmark. You can incarnate yourself as often as you like, but repeated direct intercessions in the affairs of mortals typically attract the attention and sanction of older and more established deities.
name: Assume Godhood

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 135 **Archetype** [Prophesied Monarch](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=294) --- Your Calling has culminated in a destiny of rulership, and you’re able to forge a mythic bond with noble knights whose destinies become intertwined with your own. Designate up to five allies as your knights; as long as you’re conscious, your knights’ [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67) condition can’t rise above 3, and if they’re ever in mortal peril (such as being [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) with the [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=69) condition or imprisoned in an enemy fortress), you instantly receive a vision revealing their location and condition. In addition, you gain the Royal Grace exploration activity. **Royal Grace** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [exploration](/Skills.aspx?ID=23), [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) You spend 10 minutes conferring with one of your knights who has the doomed condition. By discussing plans for your future kingdom with them and describing the benefits of your rule, you can attempt to soothe their spirits. Attempt a [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) or [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) check against a [hard DC of your knight's level](/Rules.aspx?ID=2629). Regardless of the result, that knight gains the benefits of the [Refocus](/Actions.aspx?ID=2621) activity (if they have a focus pool) but is temporarily immune to Royal Grace for 24 hours. **Critical Success** The knight’s doomed condition is reduced by 2 (minimum 0).
**Success** The knight’s doomed condition is reduced by 1 (minimum 0).
**Failure** The knight’s doomed condition isn’t reduced. Prophesied Monarch Dedication leads to... [Bloom of Health](/Feats.aspx?ID=7404), [Decree of Banisment](/Feats.aspx?ID=7405), [Decree of Execution](/Feats.aspx?ID=7412), [Decree of Prosperity](/Feats.aspx?ID=7406), [Decree of War](/Feats.aspx?ID=7413), [Fated Duel](/Feats.aspx?ID=7414), [Kneel Before the Rightful Heir](/Feats.aspx?ID=7408), [Legacy of Monarchs](/Feats.aspx?ID=7416), [Might of the Realm](/Feats.aspx?ID=7407), [My Kingdom, My Blood](/Feats.aspx?ID=7409), [Noble Sacrifice](/Feats.aspx?ID=7410), [Once and Future](/Feats.aspx?ID=7417), [Protect the Royal Line](/Feats.aspx?ID=7411), [Sovereign's Blade](/Feats.aspx?ID=7415)
name: Prophesied Monarch Dedication

<title
    level="1"

>
[Decree of Banisment](/Feats.aspx?ID=7405) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Auditory" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=541" />
<trait label="Incapacitation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=631" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 135

**Archetype** [Prophesied Monarch](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=294)

**Frequency**
once per day

**Prerequisites**
[Prophesied Monarch Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7403)

</column>

---

You assume a demeanor of authority and decree banishment upon a foe who has defied your authority. Speak your decree; a creature you designate within 60 feet who can hear your decree must succeed at a Will saving throw against your class DC or spell DC (whichever is higher) or spend its next turn moving as far away from you as it possibly can; this includes using magic or special movement modes, if it has any, to expedite its retreat. 

If the target attempts to move back within 60 feet of you at any time in the next week, it must succeed at a Will saving throw against your class DC or spell DC (whichever is higher) or the attempt fails, any movement ends, and any actions or spell slots used in the attempt (such as if the target were attempting to magically approach you) are wasted.

When you speak a Decree of Banishment, you can spend 1 [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320) as part of the action to have all friendly creatures and even the land itself within a 10-mile radius of you at that moment to reject the banished target. Within the affected area for the next month, the target has a –4 penalty to all [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) checks to [Gather Information](/Actions.aspx?ID=2391) or [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=2392), all [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) checks to [Coerce](/Actions.aspx?ID=2394), and all [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) checks to [Subsist](/Actions.aspx?ID=2368). If the target defies your Decree of Banishment and continues to operate within the area, you hear about their activities the next time you encounter a group of friendly creatures with 10 or more members. You can have only one target affected by this month-long banishment at a time; if you inflict a month-long banishment on another target, the effects on the first target end.
name: Decree of Banisment

You speak with a gentle encouragement that inspires confidence and competence in your followers. Speak your decree; all your knights who hear your decree treat failures (but not critical failures) on non-[attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540) skill checks as successes for 1 minute.

When you speak a Decree of Prosperity, you can spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320) as part of the action to have the inspiration spread to every individual within a 10-mile radius around you at that moment. Each individual receives a +2 status bonus to all checks to [Earn Income](/Rules.aspx?ID=2635), and they’re all able to find jobs of their level in the region for the next month. The people who benefit from this effect quickly learn that it happened because of your decree; for the next month, you can obtain food and lodging for free anywhere within the affected area, and you can obtain up to 10 common items of 3rd-level or lower at half price.
name: Decree of Prosperity

All enemies within 40 feet who can see and hear you must attempt a Will saving throw against your class DC or spell DC, whichever is higher. Regardless of the result, a creature is then temporarily immune to Kneel Before the Rightful Heir for 24 hours. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes 3d6 [persistent mental damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86).
**Failure** The creature takes 6d6 persistent [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) damage.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes 6d6 persistent mental damage and is [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 2 for as long as it’s taking that persistent damage. The persistent mental damage can be ended only if the creature [Drops Prone](/Actions.aspx?ID=2295) in a location that you can see and doesn't Stand until their next turn. An affected creature is aware of this restriction.
name: Kneel Before the Rightful Heir

>
[My Kingdom, My Blood](/Feats.aspx?ID=7409) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=645" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 136

**Archetype** [Prophesied Monarch](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=294)

**Prerequisites**
[Prophesied Monarch Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7403)

</column>

---

The health of the ruler is the health of the kingdom, and the health of the kingdom is that of its ruler, each bound to the other inextricably. [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305). You can [Burrow](/Actions.aspx?ID=2310), [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=2374), [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312), or [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=2381) instead of Striding if you have the corresponding movement type. You then plant your fist or weapon into the ground at your feet, creating a 60-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) in an aura around you that lasts for 1 minute. Whenever an ally within this aura takes damage, you can use your reaction to take half the damage in their stead while they take the remainder. When you take damage through this link, you don’t apply any [resistances](/Rules.aspx?ID=2893), weaknesses, or other abilities you have to that damage; you simply take that amount of damage. Whenever you take damage while this effect is active, an ally within the aura can use their reaction to take half the damage for you in the same manner.

The effect ends immediately if you’re reduced to 0 Hit Points.
name: My Kingdom, My Blood

>
[Noble Sacrifice](/Feats.aspx?ID=7410) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 136

**Archetype** [Prophesied Monarch](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=294)

**Prerequisites**
[Prophesied Monarch Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7403)

**Trigger**
One of your designated knights is within 15 feet of you and would take damage from an enemy’s [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306).

</column>

---

As your knights devote their lives and swords to you, so too do you return their devotion with your own fierce protection. Spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320). The knight [Steps](/Actions.aspx?ID=2304) as a free action, and you [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) into the space previously occupied by the knight; this movement ignores [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) and greater difficult terrain. You then take the triggering damage instead. If you have a [shield raised](/Actions.aspx?ID=2316) and have the [Shield Block](/Feats.aspx?ID=5212) reaction, you can use it as part of this ability, applying damage to the shield as though you were the original target of the attack. The enemy whose [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) triggered this reaction is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) until the end of your next turn.
name: Noble Sacrifice

>
[Protect the Royal Line](/Feats.aspx?ID=7411) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 136

**Archetype** [Prophesied Monarch](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=294)

**Prerequisites**
[Prophesied Monarch Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7403)

**Requirements**
You’re [flanked](/Rules.aspx?ID=2375) by at least two enemies.

</column>

---

You must keep your royal person safe from harm, especially when surrounded by those who would dare approach you. Attempt an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check to [Reposition](/Actions.aspx?ID=2379) or [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=2380) one of the enemies who’s [flanking](/Rules.aspx?ID=2375) you. If you can see at least one of your designated knights, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to this check; this bonus increases to +4 if one of your designated knights is adjacent to you. If you succeed, you get a critical success. If your roll would’ve been a critical success, you increase the distance you Reposition or Shove the enemy by 5 feet.
name: Protect the Royal Line
requirement_markdown: You’re [flanked](/Rules.aspx?ID=2375) by at least two enemies.

markdown:
**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 136 **Archetype** [Prophesied Monarch](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=294) **Frequency** once per day **Prerequisites** [Prophesied Monarch Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7403) --- You proclaim that a foe has violated the sanctity of your realm and must die. Speak your decree; a creature you designate within 60 feet who can hear your decree must attempt a Will saving throw against your class DC or spell DC, whichever is higher. Once targeted, the creature is temporarily immune for 1 year. Whether the target perishes or is marked for death depends on their level and the result of their saving throw. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** If the target is 14th level or lower, it drops to 1 Hit point. If the target is 15th level or higher, it takes 50 spirit damage.
**Failure** If the target is 14th level or lower, it dies instantly. If the target is 15th level or higher, it takes 50 spirit damage; if this damage brings it to 0 Hit Points, it dies instantly. Otherwise, it gains [weakness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2317) 20 to all damage for 1 minute.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but a target who survives the damage is [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) 1 for 1 minute. When you speak a Decree of Execution, you can spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320) as part of the action to remove the incapacitation trait.
name: Decree of Execution

    level="1"
    right="Feat 18"
    pfs=""
>
[Decree of War](/Feats.aspx?ID=7413) <actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Auditory" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=541" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 137

**Archetype** [Prophesied Monarch](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=294)

**Frequency**
once per day

**Prerequisites**
[Prophesied Monarch Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7403)

</column>

---

You declare your current foes as irredeemable enemies, calling your allies down upon them. Speak your decree; all your knights who hear your decree can use their reaction to [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) up to twice directly toward an enemy and then [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) that enemy if it’s within their reach or range.

When you speak a Decree of War, you can spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320) as part of the action to let every individual within a 10-mile radius around you at that moment know that war has come to your kingdom. You and your knights are able to gain access to free food, lodging, and repairs of common items while within the affected area for the next month, as allied citizens and creatures come to your aid. During this time, you also receive at least 10-minutes’ warning whenever an enemy group of more than 10 creatures travels in your direction, as allied scouts report to you.
name: Decree of War

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 18"
    pfs=""
>
[Fated Duel](/Feats.aspx?ID=7414) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 137

**Archetype** [Prophesied Monarch](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=294)

**Frequency**
once per week

**Prerequisites**
[Prophesied Monarch Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7403)

</column>

---

You and your knights are an unbeatable force together, but sometimes it’s a monarch’s duty to resolve the fate of their kingdom with nothing more than their own trusted weapon. Spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320), and choose one opponent within 30 feet; you declare a Fated Duel against that opponent. 

If the opponent accepts your challenge, you’re locked in mystical combat together. Neither you nor your opponent can move more than 30 feet from each other; you both can’t be harmed by any creature other than each other; and you both can’t harm any creature other than each other. This mystical bond is incredibly draining on both parties, pulling at their life and spirit; if neither you nor your opponent has defeated the other by the time 1 minute has passed, you both become [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67) 1. Your doomed conditions increase by 1 for each additional minute that passes until one or both of you die.

If your opponent refuses your challenge, you regain a Mythic Point and can immediately attempt an [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) check at mythic proficiency to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=2395) them.
name: Fated Duel

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Light" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=640" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />

</column>

---

As you raise your weapon into the air, it begins to shed a [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2402) that pierces [darkness](/Traits.aspx?ID=569) and calls your knights to your side. Spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320); you automatically [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) any magical darkness whose counteract rank is 7 or lower. Any knight who can see your raised weapon can immediately use their reaction to [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) up to twice directly toward you, and any of your knights who are adjacent to you after this movement get a +2 status bonus to their attack rolls until the start of your next turn.
name: Sovereign's Blade

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 20"
    pfs=""
>
[Legacy of Monarchs](/Feats.aspx?ID=7416) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 137

**Archetype** [Prophesied Monarch](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=294)

**Prerequisites**
[Prophesied Monarch Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7403)

</column>

---

No matter what might happen to you, your legacy shall never die. If you’re slain, any of your knights who touches your corpse can choose to take up your mantle; over the course of 10 minutes, they don your equipment, replace their attribute modifiers with yours, replace their skills and feats with yours, and become you for all intents and purposes, retaining only their [ancestry](/Rules.aspx?ID=3286), [heritage](/Rules.aspx?ID=2083), and ancestry feats. If you’re later returned to life, you can choose to reclaim your mantle from your former knight; if you do so, they revert to their previous state, and you reclaim all that was yours. Otherwise, you can choose to live a new life, retraining all your skills and feats while adopting a new name and identity.
name: Legacy of Monarchs

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 20"
    pfs=""
>
[Once and Future](/Feats.aspx?ID=7417) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 137

**Archetype** [Prophesied Monarch](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=294)

**Prerequisites**
[Prophesied Monarch Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7403)

</column>

---

Though foul treachery might lay you low or dark magic still your heart, when the people cry out for you in desperate need, you shall rise to protect them. You can’t die except due to old age as long as one of your knights is alive and within 30 feet; if your [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=69) or [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67) condition would increase to a high enough value to kill you, it doesn’t increase, and any effect that would instantly kill you instead just reduces you to 0 Hit Points. The first time each year you would die for any reason other than old age, you instead return to life and consciousness with a number of Hit Points equal to twice your level and gain a Mythic Point. If you ever truly die and aren’t returned to life within 1 year, you’re reborn as a child into a new mortal body somewhere in the world where stories of your accomplishments are still told. You don’t complete your journey along the River of Souls until all mortal memory of you has vanished.
name: Once and Future

<title level="2">Wildspell Dedication leads to...</title>
[Burning Surge](/Feats.aspx?ID=7423), [Extend Surge](/Feats.aspx?ID=7419), [Galvanize Spell](/Feats.aspx?ID=7429), [Imbue Spell](/Feats.aspx?ID=7424), [Invigorating Surge](/Feats.aspx?ID=7420), [Mana Detonation](/Feats.aspx?ID=7430), [Mystic Life Force](/Feats.aspx?ID=7432), [Mystical Flare](/Feats.aspx?ID=7425), [Mythic Counter](/Feats.aspx?ID=7431), [Mythic Heightening](/Feats.aspx?ID=7421), [Rapid Switch](/Feats.aspx?ID=7426), [Recharging Transference](/Feats.aspx?ID=7427), [Spell Network](/Feats.aspx?ID=7422), [Surging Interference](/Feats.aspx?ID=7433), [Thundering Surge](/Feats.aspx?ID=7428)
name: Wildspell Dedication

These temporary Hit Points last for as long as the target is in the aura. Using this ability again cancels the temporary Hit Points from any previous usage. If you spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320) as part of this action, you grant the temporary Hit Points to a number of creatures up to half your level that are within the aura.

Any spell attack roll is made with mythic proficiency, and the spell’s DC is calculated with mythic proficiency. The spell slot used to cast the spell isn’t changed.
